built with pdoc

seclai

Seclai Python SDK.

Provides synchronous and asynchronous clients for the Seclai API.

Quick start::

from seclai import Seclai

client = Seclai(api_key="...")
agents = client.list_agents()

All public symbols are re-exported from this package:

  • Seclai — synchronous client
  • AsyncSeclai — asynchronous client
  • SeclaiError — base exception
  • SeclaiConfigurationError — configuration errors
  • SeclaiAPIStatusError — non-2xx HTTP responses
  • SeclaiAPIValidationError — HTTP 422 validation errors
  • SeclaiStreamingError — SSE stream errors
  • AgentRunStreamRequest — typed request body for streaming runs
  • JSONValue — recursive JSON type alias
 1"""Seclai Python SDK.
 2
 3Provides synchronous and asynchronous clients for the Seclai API.
 4
 5Quick start::
 6
 7    from seclai import Seclai
 8
 9    client = Seclai(api_key="...")
10    agents = client.list_agents()
11
12All public symbols are re-exported from this package:
13
14- :class:`Seclai` — synchronous client
15- :class:`AsyncSeclai` — asynchronous client
16- :class:`SeclaiError` — base exception
17- :class:`SeclaiConfigurationError` — configuration errors
18- :class:`SeclaiAPIStatusError` — non-2xx HTTP responses
19- :class:`SeclaiAPIValidationError` — HTTP 422 validation errors
20- :class:`SeclaiStreamingError` — SSE stream errors
21- :class:`AgentRunStreamRequest` — typed request body for streaming runs
22- :data:`JSONValue` — recursive JSON type alias
23"""
24
25from .auth import (
26    DEFAULT_SSO_CLIENT_ID,
27    DEFAULT_SSO_DOMAIN,
28    DEFAULT_SSO_REGION,
29    SsoCacheEntry,
30    SsoProfile,
31)
32from .seclai import (
33    AgentRunStreamRequest,
34    AsyncSeclai,
35    JSONValue,
36    Seclai,
37    SeclaiAPIStatusError,
38    SeclaiAPIValidationError,
39    SeclaiConfigurationError,
40    SeclaiError,
41    SeclaiStreamingError,
42)
43

45    "AgentRunStreamRequest",

class AgentRunStreamRequest(typing.TypedDict): 
81class AgentRunStreamRequest(TypedDict):
82    """Request body for streaming agent runs.
83
84    This matches the API schema for POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream.
85    """
86
87    input: str | None
88    metadata: dict[str, JSONValue]

Request body for streaming agent runs.

This matches the API schema for POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream.

input: str | None
metadata: dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | list[dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | str | int | float | bool | None]

class AsyncSeclai(seclai.seclai._SeclaiBase): 
3879class AsyncSeclai(_SeclaiBase):
3880    """Seclai asynchronous client.
3881
3882    Most users should prefer the typed convenience methods (e.g. `run_agent`, `list_sources`)
3883    which raise SDK exceptions on failures.
3884
3885    This client can be used as an async context manager:
3886
3887        async with AsyncSeclai(api_key="...") as client:
3888            sources = await client.list_sources()
3889    """
3890
3891    def __init__(
3892        self,
3893        *,
3894        api_key: str | None = None,
3895        access_token: str | Callable[[], str | Awaitable[str]] | None = None,
3896        timeout: float = 30.0,
3897        api_key_header: str = "x-api-key",
3898        default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3899        http_client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None,
3900        profile: str | None = None,
3901        config_dir: str | None = None,
3902        auto_refresh: bool = True,
3903        account_id: str | None = None,
3904    ) -> None:
3905        """Create an asynchronous Seclai client.
3906
3907        Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):
3908
3909        1. Explicit ``api_key``
3910        2. Explicit ``access_token`` (static string or provider callable)
3911        3. ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` environment variable
3912        4. SSO profile from ``~/.seclai/config`` + cached tokens
3913
3914        Args:
3915            api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` is used.
3916            access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one
3917                (sync or async). Mutually exclusive with ``api_key``.
3918            timeout: Request timeout (seconds).
3919            api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key.
3920            default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request.
3921            http_client: Optional pre-configured ``httpx.AsyncClient`` to use.
3922            profile: SSO profile name from ``~/.seclai/config``.
3923            config_dir: Override the config directory path.
3924            auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to ``True``.
3925            account_id: Target organization account ID (``X-Account-Id`` header).
3926
3927        Raises:
3928            SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.
3929        """
3930        super().__init__(
3931            api_key=api_key,
3932            access_token=access_token,
3933            timeout=timeout,
3934            api_key_header=api_key_header,
3935            default_headers=default_headers,
3936            profile=profile,
3937            config_dir=config_dir,
3938            auto_refresh=auto_refresh,
3939            account_id=account_id,
3940        )
3941        self._client = http_client or httpx.AsyncClient(
3942            base_url=SECLAI_API_URL,
3943            timeout=self._options.timeout,
3944            headers=self._default_headers(),
3945        )
3946        self._owns_client = http_client is None
3947
3948    async def aclose(self) -> None:
3949        """Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.
3950
3951        If you created the client with a custom ``http_client``, this is a no-op
3952        for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).
3953        """
3954        if self._owns_client:
3955            await self._client.aclose()
3956        if self._owns_generated_client and self._generated_client_instance is not None:
3957            await self._generated_client_instance.get_async_httpx_client().aclose()
3958
3959    async def __aenter__(self) -> Self:
3960        """Enter an async context manager and return self."""
3961        return self
3962
3963    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type: object, exc: object, tb: object) -> None:
3964        """Exit the async context manager and close resources.
3965
3966        Args:
3967            exc_type: Exception type (if any).
3968            exc: Exception instance (if any).
3969            tb: Traceback (if any).
3970        """
3971        await self.aclose()
3972
3973    async def request(
3974        self,
3975        method: str,
3976        path: str,
3977        *,
3978        params: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
3979        json: JSONValue | None = None,
3980        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3981    ) -> JSONValue | str | None:
3982        """Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.
3983
3984        This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed
3985        convenience methods on this client.
3986
3987        Args:
3988            method: HTTP method (e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`).
3989            path: Request path relative to `SECLAI_API_URL` (e.g. `"/sources/"`).
3990            params: Query parameters.
3991            json: JSON body to send.
3992            headers: Per-request header overrides.
3993
3994        Returns:
3995            Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or `None` for empty bodies.
3996
3997        Raises:
3998            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.
3999        """
4000        response = await self._client.request(
4001            method,
4002            path,
4003            params=params,
4004            json=json,
4005            headers=await _merge_request_headers_async(
4006                options=self._options, request_headers=headers
4007            ),
4008        )
4009        _raise_for_status(response)
4010        if not response.content:
4011            return None
4012        content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
4013        if "application/json" in content_type:
4014            return cast(JSONValue, response.json())
4015        return response.text
4016
4017    async def run_agent(self, agent_id: str, body: AgentRunRequest) -> AgentRunResponse:
4018        """Run an agent.
4019
4020        Starts a new agent run for the given agent and returns the run record.
4021
4022        Args:
4023            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4024            body: Agent run request payload.
4025
4026        Returns:
4027            The created agent run.
4028
4029        Raises:
4030            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
4031            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
4032        """
4033        from seclai._generated.api.agents.run_agent_api_agents_agent_id_runs_post import (
4034            asyncio_detailed,
4035        )
4036
4037        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs"
4038        response = await asyncio_detailed(
4039            agent_id=agent_id, client=(await self._async_generated_client()), body=body
4040        )
4041        self._raise_for_openapi_response(
4042            method="POST",
4043            path=path,
4044            response=response,
4045            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
4046        )
4047        if response.parsed is None:
4048            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4049                message="Empty response",
4050                status_code=int(response.status_code),
4051                method="POST",
4052                url=self._build_url(path),
4053                response_text=None,
4054            )
4055        parsed = response.parsed
4056        if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
4057            raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
4058                message="Validation error",
4059                status_code=int(response.status_code),
4060                method="POST",
4061                url=self._build_url(path),
4062                response_text=None,
4063                validation_error=parsed,
4064            )
4065        return parsed
4066
4067    async def run_streaming_agent_and_wait(
4068        self,
4069        agent_id: str,
4070        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
4071        *,
4072        timeout: float | None = None,
4073        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
4074    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
4075        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion (async).
4076
4077        This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE).
4078        The method returns when an `event: done` message is received.
4079        """
4080        import json as _json
4081        import time as _time
4082
4083        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
4084
4085        merged_headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
4086            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
4087        )
4088        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
4089
4090        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
4091        start = _time.monotonic()
4092
4093        try:
4094            async with self._client.stream(
4095                "POST",
4096                path,
4097                json=body,
4098                headers=merged_headers,
4099                timeout=timeout_seconds,
4100            ) as response:
4101                if response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY:
4102                    payload = response.json()
4103                    raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
4104                        message=f"Request failed with status {response.status_code}",
4105                        status_code=int(response.status_code),
4106                        method="POST",
4107                        url=self._build_url(path),
4108                        response_text=response.text,
4109                        validation_error=HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload),
4110                    )
4111                _raise_for_status(response)
4112
4113                current_event: str | None = None
4114                data_lines: list[str] = []
4115                last_seen: AgentRunResponse | None = None
4116
4117                def dispatch() -> AgentRunResponse | None:
4118                    nonlocal current_event, data_lines, last_seen
4119                    if current_event is None and not data_lines:
4120                        return None
4121                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
4122                    evt = current_event
4123                    current_event = None
4124                    data_lines = []
4125                    if not data:
4126                        return None
4127                    if evt not in {"init", "done"}:
4128                        return None
4129                    try:
4130                        parsed_dict = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
4131                        parsed = AgentRunResponse.from_dict(parsed_dict)
4132                        last_seen = parsed
4133                        if evt == "done":
4134                            return parsed
4135                    except Exception:
4136                        return None
4137                    return None
4138
4139                async for line in response.aiter_lines():
4140                    if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
4141                        raise SeclaiError(
4142                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
4143                        )
4144
4145                    if line == "":
4146                        done = dispatch()
4147                        if done is not None:
4148                            return done
4149                        continue
4150                    if line.startswith(":"):
4151                        continue
4152                    if line.startswith("event:"):
4153                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
4154                        continue
4155                    if line.startswith("data:"):
4156                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
4157                        continue
4158
4159                done = dispatch()
4160                if done is not None:
4161                    return done
4162                if last_seen is not None:
4163                    return last_seen
4164                raise SeclaiError("Stream ended before receiving a 'done' event.")
4165        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
4166            raise SeclaiError(
4167                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
4168            ) from e
4169
4170    async def list_agent_runs(
4171        self, agent_id: str, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50
4172    ) -> AgentRunListResponse:
4173        """List agent runs.
4174
4175        Returns paginated runs for a single agent.
4176
4177        Args:
4178            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4179            page: Page number (1-indexed).
4180            limit: Items per page.
4181
4182        Returns:
4183            A paginated list of runs.
4184
4185        Raises:
4186            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
4187            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
4188        """
4189        from seclai._generated.api.agents.list_agent_runs_api_agents_agent_id_runs_get import (
4190            asyncio_detailed,
4191        )
4192

4582    async def upload_file_to_source(
4583        self,
4584        source_connection_id: str,
4585        *,
4586        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
4587        title: str | None = None,
4588        metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
4589        file_name: str | None = None,
4590        mime_type: str | None = None,
4591    ) -> FileUploadResponse:
4592        """Upload a file to a specific source connection.
4593
4594        Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.
4595
4596        Maximum file size: 200 MiB.
4597
4598        Supported MIME types:
4599            - `application/epub+zip`
4600            - `application/json`
4601            - `application/msword`
4602            - `application/pdf`
4603            - `application/vnd.ms-excel`
4604            - `application/vnd.ms-outlook`
4605            - `application/vnd.ms-powerpoint`
4606            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation`
4607            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`
4608            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`
4609            - `application/xml`
4610            - `application/zip`
4611            - `audio/flac`
4612            - `audio/mp4`
4613            - `audio/mpeg`
4614            - `audio/ogg`
4615            - `audio/wav`
4616            - `image/bmp`
4617            - `image/gif`
4618            - `image/jpeg`
4619            - `image/png`
4620            - `image/tiff`
4621            - `image/webp`
4622            - `text/csv`
4623            - `text/html`
4624            - `text/markdown` / `text/x-markdown`
4625            - `text/plain`
4626            - `text/xml`
4627            - `video/mp4`
4628            - `video/quicktime`
4629            - `video/x-msvideo`
4630
4631        If `mime_type` is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it.
4632        If the upload is sent as `application/octet-stream`, the server attempts to infer
4633        the type from the file extension.
4634
4635        Notes:
4636            - If `file` is a path or `bytes`, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and
4637              closes it before returning.
4638            - If `file` is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.
4639
4640        Args:
4641            source_connection_id: Source connection identifier.
4642            file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like.
4643            title: Optional title for the uploaded file.
4644            metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content.
4645            file_name: Optional filename to send when `file` is bytes or a file-like.
4646            mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.
4647
4648        Returns:
4649            Upload response details.
4650
4651        Raises:
4652            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
4653            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
4654        """
4655        import json as _json
4656
4657        from seclai._generated.models.body_upload_file_to_source_api_sources_source_connection_id_upload_post import (
4658            BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost,
4659        )
4660        from seclai._generated.types import UNSET, File
4661
4662        created_payload: BinaryIO | None = None
4663        try:
4664            upload_file: File
4665            if isinstance(file, File):
4666                upload_file = file
4667            elif isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
4668                file_path = Path(file)
4669                resolved_file_name = file_name or file_path.name
4670                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_file_name)
4671                created_payload = file_path.open("rb")
4672                upload_file = File(
4673                    payload=created_payload,
4674                    file_name=resolved_file_name,
4675                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4676                )
4677            elif isinstance(file, bytes):
4678                created_payload = BytesIO(file)
4679                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
4680                upload_file = File(
4681                    payload=created_payload,
4682                    file_name=file_name,
4683                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4684                )
4685            else:
4686                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
4687                upload_file = File(
4688                    payload=file,
4689                    file_name=file_name,
4690                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4691                )
4692
4693            body = BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost(
4694                file=upload_file,
4695                title=UNSET if title is None else title,
4696            )
4697
4698            if metadata is not None:
4699                body["metadata"] = _json.dumps(metadata)
4700
4701            endpoint_path = f"/sources/{source_connection_id}/upload"
4702
4703            http = (await self._async_generated_client()).get_async_httpx_client()
4704            raw = await http.request(
4705                "POST",
4706                endpoint_path,
4707                files=body.to_multipart(),
4708            )
4709
4710            parsed: FileUploadResponse | HTTPValidationError | None = None
4711            if raw.status_code == 200:
4712                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
4713                parsed = FileUploadResponse.from_dict(payload)
4714            elif raw.status_code == 422:
4715                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
4716                parsed = HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload)
4717
4718            response = OpenAPIResponse(
4719                status_code=HTTPStatus(raw.status_code),
4720                content=raw.content,
4721                headers=raw.headers,
4722                parsed=parsed,
4723            )
4724            self._raise_for_openapi_response(
4725                method="POST",
4726                path=endpoint_path,
4727                response=response,
4728                ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
4729            )
4730            if response.parsed is None:
4731                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4732                    message="Empty response",
4733                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
4734                    method="POST",
4735                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
4736                    response_text=None,
4737                )
4738            parsed_response = cast(
4739                FileUploadResponse | HTTPValidationError, response.parsed
4740            )
4741            if isinstance(parsed_response, HTTPValidationError):
4742                raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
4743                    message="Validation error",
4744                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
4745                    method="POST",
4746                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
4747                    response_text=None,
4748                    validation_error=parsed_response,
4749                )
4750            if not isinstance(parsed_response, FileUploadResponse):
4751                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4752                    message="Unexpected response shape",
4753                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
4754                    method="POST",
4755                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
4756                    response_text=None,
4757                )
4758            return parsed_response
4759        finally:
4760            if created_payload is not None:
4761                created_payload.close()

4849                ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
4850            )
4851            if response.parsed is None:
4852                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4853                    message="Empty response",
4854                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
4855                    method="POST",
4856                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
4857                    response_text=None,
4858                )
4859            parsed = response.parsed
4860            if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
4861                raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
4862                    message="Validation error",
4863                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
4864                    method="POST",
4865                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
4866                    response_text=None,
4867                    validation_error=parsed,
4868                )
4869            return parsed
4870        finally:
4871            if created_payload is not None:
4872                created_payload.close()
4873
4874    # ── Agents ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4875
4876    async def list_agents(self, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50) -> dict[str, Any]:
4877        """List all agents.
4878
4879        Args:
4880            page: Page number (1-indexed).
4881            limit: Items per page.
4882
4883        Returns:
4884            Paginated list of agents.
4885        """
4886        return cast(
4887            dict[str, Any],
4888            await self.request(
4889                "GET",
4890                "/agents",
4891                params=_strip_none({"page": page, "limit": limit}),
4892            ),
4893        )
4894
4895    async def create_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
4896        """Create a new agent.
4897
4898        Args:
4899            body: Agent creation payload (name, description, etc.).
4900
4901        Returns:
4902            The created agent.
4903        """
4904        return cast(dict[str, Any], await self.request("POST", "/agents", json=body))
4905
4906    async def get_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
4907        """Get an agent by ID.
4908
4909        Args:
4910            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4911
4912        Returns:
4913            Agent details.
4914        """
4915        return cast(dict[str, Any], await self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}"))
4916
4917    async def update_agent(self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
4918        """Update an agent.
4919
4920        Args:
4921            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4922            body: Fields to update.
4923
4924        Returns:
4925            The updated agent.
4926        """
4927        return cast(
4928            dict[str, Any], await self.request("PUT", f"/agents/{agent_id}", json=body)
4929        )
4930
4931    async def delete_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> None:
4932        """Delete an agent.
4933
4934        Args:
4935            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4936        """
4937        await self.request("DELETE", f"/agents/{agent_id}")
4938
4939    # ── Agent Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4940
4941    async def export_agent(
4942        self, agent_id: str, *, download: bool = True
4943    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
4944        """Export an agent definition as a portable JSON snapshot.
4945
4946        Args:
4947            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4948            download: When True (default), the server sets Content-Disposition: attachment.
4949
4950        Returns:
4951            The exported agent snapshot.
4952        """
4953        params = _strip_none({"download": download})
4954        return cast(
4955            dict[str, Any],
4956            await self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/export", params=params),
4957        )
4958
4959    async def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
4960        """Validate an ``agent_definition`` payload without creating an agent.
4961
4962        Use before :meth:`create_agent` or :meth:`update_agent` with an
4963        ``agent_definition`` to surface ``unresolved_refs`` — workflow
4964        references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or
4965        sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned
4966        ids back in ``entity_remap`` on the commit call to substitute them.
4967
4968        Args:
4969            body: ``{"agent_definition": ...}`` — same shape produced by
4970                :meth:`export_agent`.
4971
4972        Returns:
4973            Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any
4974            ``unresolved_refs``.
4975
4976        Raises:
4977            SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including
4978                HTTP 422 when the ``agent_definition`` fails validation.
4979                On 422 the response body (available as
4980                ``SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text``) is an
4981                ``AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse`` listing each field
4982                error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical
4983                ``source`` echo.
4984        """
4985        return cast(
4986            dict[str, Any],
4987            await self.request("POST", "/agents/preview-import", json=body),
4988        )
4989
4990    # ── Agent Definitions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4991
4992    async def get_agent_definition(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
4993        """Get the step definition for an agent.
4994
4995        Args:
4996            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4997
4998        Returns:
4999            The agent's step definition.
5000        """
5001        return cast(
5002            dict[str, Any], await self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition")
5003        )
5004
5005    async def update_agent_definition(
5006        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5007    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5008        """Update the step definition for an agent.
5009
5010        Args:
5011            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5012            body: New definition payload (must include ``change_id`` for optimistic locking).
5013
5014        Returns:
5015            The updated agent definition.
5016        """
5017        return cast(
5018            dict[str, Any],
5019            await self.request(
5020                "PUT",
5021                f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition",
5022                json=body,
5023            ),
5024        )
5025
5026    # ── Agent Runs (additional) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
5027
5028    async def search_agent_runs(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
5029        """Search agent runs across agents.
5030
5031        Args:
5032            body: Search request with query filters.
5033
5034        Returns:
5035            Search results with matching runs.
5036        """
5037        return cast(
5038            dict[str, Any], await self.request("POST", "/agents/runs/search", json=body)
5039        )
5040
5041    async def cancel_agent_run(self, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
5042        """Cancel an in-progress agent run.
5043
5044        Args:
5045            run_id: Run identifier.
5046
5047        Returns:
5048            The updated agent run.
5049        """
5050        return cast(
5051            dict[str, Any], await self.request("POST", f"/agents/runs/{run_id}/cancel")
5052        )
5053
5054    # ── Agent Input Uploads ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
5055
5056    async def upload_agent_input(
5057        self,
5058        agent_id: str,
5059        *,
5060        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
5061        file_name: str | None = None,
5062        mime_type: str | None = None,
5063    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5064        """Upload a file as input for an agent run.
5065
5066        Args:
5067            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5068            file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like).
5069            file_name: Filename to send.
5070            mime_type: MIME type of the file.
5071
5072        Returns:
5073            Upload response with the input upload ID.
5074        """
5075        payload, created = Seclai._prepare_upload_payload(
5076            self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # reuse sync helper from async context
5077            file,
5078            file_name,
5079            mime_type,
5080        )
5081        try:
5082            response = await self._client.post(
5083                f"/agents/{agent_id}/upload-input",
5084                files={"file": payload},
5085                headers=await _merge_request_headers_async(
5086                    options=self._options, request_headers=None
5087                ),
5088            )
5089            _raise_for_status(response)
5090            return cast(dict[str, Any], response.json())
5091        finally:
5092            if created is not None:
5093                created.close()
5094
5095    async def get_agent_input_upload_status(
5096        self, agent_id: str, upload_id: str
5097    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5098        """Get the status of an agent input upload.
5099
5100        Args:
5101            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5102            upload_id: Upload identifier.
5103
5104        Returns:
5105            Upload status details.
5106        """
5107        return cast(
5108            dict[str, Any],
5109            await self.request(
5110                "GET",
5111                f"/agents/{agent_id}/input-uploads/{upload_id}",
5112            ),
5113        )
5114
5115    # ── Agent AI Assistant ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5116
5117    async def generate_agent_steps(
5118        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5119    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5120        """Use the AI assistant to generate agent steps.
5121
5122        Args:
5123            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5124            body: Prompt and context for step generation.
5125
5126        Returns:
5127            Generated steps suggestion.
5128        """
5129        return cast(
5130            dict[str, Any],
5131            await self.request(
5132                "POST",
5133                f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/generate-steps",
5134                json=body,
5135            ),
5136        )
5137
5138    async def generate_step_config(
5139        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5140    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5141        """Use the AI assistant to generate configuration for a single step.
5142
5143        Args:
5144            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5145            body: Step type and context for config generation.
5146
5147        Returns:
5148            Generated step configuration.
5149        """
5150        return cast(
5151            dict[str, Any],
5152            await self.request(
5153                "POST",
5154                f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/step-config",
5155                json=body,
5156            ),
5157        )
5158
5159    async def get_agent_ai_conversation_history(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
5160        """Get the AI assistant conversation history for an agent.
5161
5162        Args:
5163            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5164
5165        Returns:
5166            Conversation history with the AI assistant.
5167        """
5168        return cast(
5169            dict[str, Any],
5170            await self.request(
5171                "GET",
5172                f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/conversations",
5173            ),
5174        )
5175
5176    async def mark_agent_ai_suggestion(
5177        self, agent_id: str, conversation_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5178    ) -> None:
5179        """Mark an AI assistant suggestion as accepted or rejected.
5180
5181        Args:
5182            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5183            conversation_id: Conversation identifier.
5184            body: Acceptance/rejection payload.
5185        """
5186        await self.request(
5187            "PATCH",
5188            f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/{conversation_id}",
5189            json=body,
5190        )
5191

5471    # ── Knowledge Bases ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5472
5473    async def list_knowledge_bases(
5474        self,
5475        *,
5476        page: int = 1,
5477        limit: int = 50,
5478        sort: str = "created_at",
5479        order: str = "desc",
5480    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5481        """List knowledge bases.
5482
5483        Args:
5484            page: Page number (1-indexed).
5485            limit: Items per page.
5486            sort: Sort field.
5487            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).
5488
5489        Returns:
5490            Paginated list of knowledge bases.
5491        """
5492        return cast(
5493            dict[str, Any],
5494            await self.request(
5495                "GET",
5496                "/knowledge_bases",
5497                params=_strip_none(
5498                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
5499                ),
5500            ),
5501        )
5502
5503    async def create_knowledge_base(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
5504        """Create a knowledge base.
5505
5506        Args:
5507            body: Knowledge base creation payload.
5508
5509        Returns:
5510            The created knowledge base.
5511        """
5512        return cast(
5513            dict[str, Any], await self.request("POST", "/knowledge_bases", json=body)
5514        )
5515
5516    async def get_knowledge_base(self, knowledge_base_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
5517        """Get a knowledge base by ID.
5518

5520            knowledge_base_id: Knowledge base identifier.
5521
5522        Returns:
5523            Knowledge base details.
5524        """

5526            dict[str, Any],
5527            await self.request(
5528                "GET",
5529                f"/knowledge_bases/{knowledge_base_id}",
5530            ),
5531        )
5532
5533    async def update_knowledge_base(
5534        self, knowledge_base_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5535    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5536        """Update a knowledge base.
5537
5538        Args:
5539            knowledge_base_id: Knowledge base identifier.
5540            body: Fields to update.
5541
5542        Returns:
5543            The updated knowledge base.
5544        """
5545        return cast(
5546            dict[str, Any],
5547            await self.request(
5548                "PUT",
5549                f"/knowledge_bases/{knowledge_base_id}",
5550                json=body,
5551            ),
5552        )

5554    async def delete_knowledge_base(self, knowledge_base_id: str) -> None:
5555        """Delete a knowledge base.

5562    # ── Memory Banks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5563
5564    async def list_memory_banks(
5565        self,
5566        *,
5567        page: int = 1,
5568        limit: int = 50,
5569        sort: str = "created_at",
5570        order: str = "desc",
5571    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5572        """List memory banks.
5573
5574        Args:
5575            page: Page number (1-indexed).
5576            limit: Items per page.
5577            sort: Sort field.
5578            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).
5579
5580        Returns:
5581            Paginated list of memory banks.
5582        """
5583        return cast(
5584            dict[str, Any],
5585            await self.request(
5586                "GET",
5587                "/memory_banks",
5588                params=_strip_none(
5589                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
5590                ),
5591            ),
5592        )
5593
5594    async def create_memory_bank(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
5595        """Create a memory bank.
5596
5597        Args:
5598            body: Memory bank creation payload (name, type, config, etc.).
5599
5600        Returns:
5601            The created memory bank.
5602        """
5603        return cast(

5864    # ── Source Exports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5865
5866    async def list_source_exports(
5867        self, source_id: str, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50
5868    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5869        """List exports for a source.
5870
5871        Args:
5872            source_id: Source identifier.
5873            page: Page number (1-indexed).
5874            limit: Items per page.
5875
5876        Returns:
5877            Paginated list of exports.
5878        """
5879        return cast(
5880            dict[str, Any],
5881            await self.request(
5882                "GET",
5883                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports",
5884                params=_strip_none({"page": page, "limit": limit}),
5885            ),
5886        )
5887
5888    async def create_source_export(
5889        self, source_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5890    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5891        """Create an export for a source.
5892
5893        Args:
5894            source_id: Source identifier.
5895            body: Export configuration (format, filters, etc.).
5896

5898            The created export.
5899        """
5900        return cast(
5901            dict[str, Any],
5902            await self.request(
5903                "POST",
5904                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports",
5905                json=body,
5906            ),
5907        )
5908
5909    async def get_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
5910        """Get details of a source export.
5911
5912        Args:
5913            source_id: Source identifier.
5914            export_id: Export identifier.
5915
5916        Returns:
5917            Export details.
5918        """
5919        return cast(
5920            dict[str, Any],
5921            await self.request(
5922                "GET",
5923                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}",
5924            ),
5925        )
5926
5927    async def cancel_source_export(
5928        self, source_id: str, export_id: str
5929    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5930        """Cancel a source export.
5931
5932        Args:
5933            source_id: Source identifier.
5934            export_id: Export identifier.
5935
5936        Returns:
5937            The updated export.
5938        """
5939        return cast(
5940            dict[str, Any],
5941            await self.request(
5942                "POST",
5943                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/cancel",
5944            ),
5945        )
5946
5947    async def delete_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> None:
5948        """Delete a source export.
5949
5950        Args:
5951            source_id: Source identifier.
5952            export_id: Export identifier.
5953        """
5954        await self.request("DELETE", f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}")
5955
5956    async def download_source_export(
5957        self, source_id: str, export_id: str
5958    ) -> httpx.Response:
5959        """Download a completed source export.
5960
5961        Returns a **streaming** response. The caller is responsible for
5962        consuming and closing the response::
5963
5964            response = await client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
5965            async with response:
5966                async for chunk in response.aiter_bytes():
5967                    f.write(chunk)
5968
5969        Args:
5970            source_id: Source identifier.
5971            export_id: Export identifier.
5972
5973        Returns:
5974            A streaming ``httpx.Response``. Must be closed by the caller.
5975        """
5976        headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
5977            options=self._options, request_headers=None
5978        )
5979        request = self._client.build_request(
5980            "GET",
5981            f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/download",
5982            headers=headers,
5983        )
5984        response = await self._client.send(request, stream=True)
5985        _raise_for_status(response)
5986        return response
5987
5988    async def estimate_source_export(
5989        self, source_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
5990    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5991        """Estimate the size/cost of a source export.
5992
5993        Args:
5994            source_id: Source identifier.
5995            body: Export estimation parameters.
5996
5997        Returns:
5998            Export estimate.
5999        """
6000        return cast(
6001            dict[str, Any],
6002            await self.request(
6003                "POST",
6004                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/estimate",
6005                json=body,
6006            ),
6007        )
6008

6091    async def list_solutions(
6092        self,
6093        *,
6094        page: int = 1,
6095        limit: int = 50,
6096        sort: str = "created_at",
6097        order: str = "desc",
6098    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
6099        """List solutions.
6100
6101        Args:
6102            page: Page number (1-indexed).
6103            limit: Items per page.
6104            sort: Sort field.
6105            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).
6106
6107        Returns:
6108            Paginated list of solutions.
6109        """
6110        return cast(
6111            dict[str, Any],
6112            await self.request(
6113                "GET",
6114                "/solutions",
6115                params=_strip_none(
6116                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
6117                ),
6118            ),
6119        )
6120

6397
6398    async def generate_solution_ai_source(
6399        self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
6400    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
6401        """Use the AI assistant to generate a source plan for a solution.
6402
6403        Args:
6404            solution_id: Solution identifier.
6405            body: Prompt and context.
6406
6407        Returns:
6408            Generated source plan.
6409        """
6410        return cast(
6411            dict[str, Any],
6412            await self.request(
6413                "POST",
6414                f"/solutions/{solution_id}/ai-assistant/source",
6415                json=body,
6416            ),
6417        )

6421    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

6430            Result of applying the plan.

6517    # ── Alerts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
6518
6519    async def list_alerts(
6520        self,
6521        *,
6522        page: int = 1,
6523        limit: int = 50,
6524        status: str | None = None,
6525        severity: str | None = None,
6526    ) -> JSONValue:
6527        """List alerts.
6528
6529        Args:
6530            page: Page number (1-indexed).
6531            limit: Items per page.
6532            status: Filter by alert status.
6533            severity: Filter by severity.
6534
6535        Returns:
6536            Paginated list of alerts.
6537        """
6538        return await self.request(
6539            "GET",
6540            "/alerts",
6541            params=_strip_none(
6542                {"page": page, "limit": limit, "status": status, "severity": severity}
6543            ),
6544        )
6545
6546    async def get_alert(self, alert_id: str) -> JSONValue:
6547        """Get an alert by ID.
6548
6549        Args:
6550            alert_id: Alert identifier.
6551
6552        Returns:
6553            Alert details.
6554        """
6555        return await self.request("GET", f"/alerts/{alert_id}")
6556

6558        self, alert_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
6559    ) -> JSONValue:

6561
6562        Args:
6563            alert_id: Alert identifier.

6567            Updated alert.
6568        """

6582

6585
6586        Args:
6587            alert_id: Alert identifier.
6588

6590            Subscription result.

6593
6594    async def unsubscribe_from_alert(self, alert_id: str) -> JSONValue:
6595        """Unsubscribe from an alert.
6596
6597        Args:
6598            alert_id: Alert identifier.
6599
6600        Returns:
6601            Unsubscription result.
6602        """
6603        return await self.request("POST", f"/alerts/{alert_id}/unsubscribe")

6793    # ── Model Playground Experiments ──────────────────────────────────────────
6794
6795    async def list_experiments(
6796        self,
6797        *,
6798        days: int | None = None,
6799        start_date: str | None = None,
6800        end_date: str | None = None,
6801        limit: int | None = None,
6802        offset: int | None = None,
6803    ) -> JSONValue:
6804        """List model playground experiments.
6805
6806        Args:
6807            days: Look-back window in days (1-730, default 30).
6808            start_date: Start date filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
6809            end_date: End date filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
6810            limit: Maximum number of results.
6811            offset: Pagination offset.
6812
6813        Returns:
6814            Paginated list of experiments.
6815        """
6816        return await self.request(
6817            "GET",
6818            "/models/playground/experiments",
6819            params=_strip_none(
6820                {
6821                    "days": days,
6822                    "start_date": start_date,
6823                    "end_date": end_date,
6824                    "limit": limit,
6825                    "offset": offset,
6826                }
6827            ),
6828        )
6829

6849        """

6853
6854    async def cancel_experiment(self, experiment_id: str) -> JSONValue:

6859
6860        Returns:
6861            Cancellation confirmation.
6862        """
6863        return await self.request(
6864            "POST", f"/models/playground/experiments/{experiment_id}/cancel"
6865        )
6866
6867    # ── Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

6892        )

7043    # ── Pagination helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7044
7045    async def paginate(
7046        self,
7047        method: str,
7048        path: str,
7049        *,
7050        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
7051        limit: int = 50,
7052        items_key: str = "data",
7053    ) -> AsyncGenerator[dict[str, Any]]:
7054        """Auto-paginate a list endpoint, yielding items lazily.
7055
7056        Fetches pages sequentially until a page returns fewer items than ``limit``
7057        or the items list is empty.  Use ``async for item in client.paginate(...):``
7058        to iterate.
7059
7060        Args:
7061            method: HTTP method (typically ``"GET"``).
7062            path: API path.
7063            params: Extra query parameters (``page`` and ``limit`` are managed automatically).
7064            limit: Items per page.
7065            items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default ``"data"``).
7066
7067        Yields:
7068            Individual items from each page.
7069        """
7070        page = 1
7071        base_params = dict(params) if params else {}
7072        while True:
7073            base_params["page"] = page
7074            base_params["limit"] = limit
7075            result = await self.request(method, path, params=base_params)
7076            if not isinstance(result, dict):
7077                break
7078            items = result.get(items_key, [])
7079            if not isinstance(items, list):
7080                break
7081            for item in items:
7082                yield cast(dict[str, Any], item)
7083            if len(items) < limit:
7084                break
7085            page += 1
7086
7087    # ── High-level Abstractions ───────────────────────────────────────────────
7088
7089    async def run_agent_and_poll(
7090        self,
7091        agent_id: str,
7092        body: AgentRunRequest,
7093        *,
7094        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
7095        include_step_outputs: bool = False,
7096    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
7097        """Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.
7098
7099        Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls ``get_agent_run`` until
7100        the status is ``"completed"`` or ``"failed"``.
7101
7102        Args:
7103            agent_id: Agent identifier.
7104            body: Agent run request payload.
7105            poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0).
7106            include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.
7107
7108        Returns:
7109            The final agent run (completed or failed).
7110
7111        Raises:
7112            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
7113            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
7114        """
7115        import asyncio
7116
7117        run = await self.run_agent(agent_id, body)
7118        while run.status not in ("completed", "failed"):
7119            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
7120            run = await self.get_agent_run(
7121                run.run_id, include_step_outputs=include_step_outputs
7122            )
7123        return run
7124
7125    async def run_streaming_agent(
7126        self,
7127        agent_id: str,
7128        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
7129        *,
7130        timeout: float | None = None,
7131        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
7132    ) -> AsyncGenerator[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]:
7133        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and yield events as they arrive.
7134
7135        Use ``async for event_type, data in client.run_streaming_agent(...)`` to
7136        process events lazily without buffering the entire stream.
7137
7138        Args:
7139            agent_id: Agent identifier.
7140            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
7141            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout.
7142            headers: Optional extra headers.
7143
7144        Yields:
7145            ``(event_type, parsed_data)`` tuples for each SSE event.
7146
7147        Raises:
7148            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
7149            SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received.
7150            SeclaiError: If the stream times out.
7151        """
7152        import json as _json
7153
7154        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
7155        merged_headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
7156            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
7157        )
7158        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
7159        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
7160        start = time.monotonic()
7161
7162        try:
7163            async with self._client.stream(
7164                "POST",
7165                path,
7166                json=body,
7167                headers=merged_headers,
7168                timeout=timeout_seconds,
7169            ) as response:
7170                _raise_for_status(response)
7171                current_event: str | None = None
7172                data_lines: list[str] = []
7173
7174                async for line in response.aiter_lines():
7175                    if time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
7176                        raise SeclaiError(
7177                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
7178                        )
7179                    if line == "":
7180                        if current_event and data_lines:
7181                            data = "\n".join(data_lines)
7182                            try:
7183                                parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
7184                            except Exception:
7185                                parsed = {"raw": data}
7186                            if current_event == "error":
7187                                raise SeclaiStreamingError(
7188                                    parsed.get("message", data),
7189                                    run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
7190                                )
7191                            yield (current_event, parsed)
7192                        current_event = None
7193                        data_lines = []
7194                        continue
7195                    if line.startswith(":"):
7196                        continue
7197                    if line.startswith("event:"):
7198                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
7199                        continue
7200                    if line.startswith("data:"):
7201                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
7202                        continue
7203
7204                if current_event and data_lines:
7205                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
7206                    try:
7207                        parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
7208                    except Exception:
7209                        parsed = {"raw": data}
7210                    if current_event == "error":
7211                        raise SeclaiStreamingError(
7212                            parsed.get("message", data),
7213                            run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
7214                        )
7215                    yield (current_event, parsed)
7216        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
7217            raise SeclaiError(
7218                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
7219            ) from e

Seclai asynchronous client.

Most users should prefer the typed convenience methods (e.g. run_agent, list_sources) which raise SDK exceptions on failures.

This client can be used as an async context manager:

async with AsyncSeclai(api_key="...") as client:
    sources = await client.list_sources()
AsyncSeclai( *, api_key: str | None = None, access_token: str | Callable[[], str | Awaitable[str]] | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, api_key_header: str = 'x-api-key', default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None, http_client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None, profile: str | None = None, config_dir: str | None = None, auto_refresh: bool = True, account_id: str | None = None) 
3891    def __init__(
3892        self,
3893        *,
3894        api_key: str | None = None,
3895        access_token: str | Callable[[], str | Awaitable[str]] | None = None,
3896        timeout: float = 30.0,
3897        api_key_header: str = "x-api-key",
3898        default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3899        http_client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None,
3900        profile: str | None = None,
3901        config_dir: str | None = None,
3902        auto_refresh: bool = True,
3903        account_id: str | None = None,
3904    ) -> None:
3905        """Create an asynchronous Seclai client.
3906
3907        Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):
3908
3909        1. Explicit ``api_key``
3910        2. Explicit ``access_token`` (static string or provider callable)
3911        3. ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` environment variable
3912        4. SSO profile from ``~/.seclai/config`` + cached tokens
3913
3914        Args:
3915            api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` is used.
3916            access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one
3917                (sync or async). Mutually exclusive with ``api_key``.
3918            timeout: Request timeout (seconds).
3919            api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key.
3920            default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request.
3921            http_client: Optional pre-configured ``httpx.AsyncClient`` to use.
3922            profile: SSO profile name from ``~/.seclai/config``.
3923            config_dir: Override the config directory path.
3924            auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to ``True``.
3925            account_id: Target organization account ID (``X-Account-Id`` header).
3926
3927        Raises:
3928            SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.
3929        """
3930        super().__init__(
3931            api_key=api_key,
3932            access_token=access_token,
3933            timeout=timeout,
3934            api_key_header=api_key_header,
3935            default_headers=default_headers,
3936            profile=profile,
3937            config_dir=config_dir,
3938            auto_refresh=auto_refresh,
3939            account_id=account_id,
3940        )
3941        self._client = http_client or httpx.AsyncClient(
3942            base_url=SECLAI_API_URL,
3943            timeout=self._options.timeout,
3944            headers=self._default_headers(),
3945        )
3946        self._owns_client = http_client is None

Create an asynchronous Seclai client.

Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):

  1. Explicit api_key
  2. Explicit access_token (static string or provider callable)
  3. SECLAI_API_KEY environment variable
  4. SSO profile from ~/.seclai/config + cached tokens

Args: api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, SECLAI_API_KEY is used. access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one (sync or async). Mutually exclusive with api_key. timeout: Request timeout (seconds). api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key. default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request. http_client: Optional pre-configured httpx.AsyncClient to use. profile: SSO profile name from ~/.seclai/config. config_dir: Override the config directory path. auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to True. account_id: Target organization account ID (X-Account-Id header).

Raises: SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.

async def aclose(self) -> None: 
3948    async def aclose(self) -> None:
3949        """Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.
3950
3951        If you created the client with a custom ``http_client``, this is a no-op
3952        for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).
3953        """
3954        if self._owns_client:
3955            await self._client.aclose()
3956        if self._owns_generated_client and self._generated_client_instance is not None:
3957            await self._generated_client_instance.get_async_httpx_client().aclose()

Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.

If you created the client with a custom http_client, this is a no-op for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).

async def request( self, method: str, path: str, *, params: Mapping[str, typing.Any] | None = None, json: dict[str, dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | list[dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | str | int | float | bool | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> dict[str, dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | list[dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | str | int | float | bool | None: 
3973    async def request(
3974        self,
3975        method: str,
3976        path: str,
3977        *,
3978        params: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
3979        json: JSONValue | None = None,
3980        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3981    ) -> JSONValue | str | None:
3982        """Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.
3983
3984        This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed
3985        convenience methods on this client.
3986
3987        Args:
3988            method: HTTP method (e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`).
3989            path: Request path relative to `SECLAI_API_URL` (e.g. `"/sources/"`).
3990            params: Query parameters.
3991            json: JSON body to send.
3992            headers: Per-request header overrides.
3993
3994        Returns:
3995            Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or `None` for empty bodies.
3996
3997        Raises:
3998            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.
3999        """
4000        response = await self._client.request(
4001            method,
4002            path,
4003            params=params,
4004            json=json,
4005            headers=await _merge_request_headers_async(
4006                options=self._options, request_headers=headers
4007            ),
4008        )
4009        _raise_for_status(response)
4010        if not response.content:
4011            return None
4012        content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
4013        if "application/json" in content_type:
4014            return cast(JSONValue, response.json())
4015        return response.text

Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.

This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed convenience methods on this client.

Args: method: HTTP method (e.g. "GET", "POST"). path: Request path relative to SECLAI_API_URL (e.g. "/sources/"). params: Query parameters. json: JSON body to send. headers: Per-request header overrides.

Returns: Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or None for empty bodies.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.

4040        )

4042            method="POST",
4043            path=path,
4044            response=response,
4045            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
4046        )
4047        if response.parsed is None:
4048            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4049                message="Empty response",
4050                status_code=int(response.status_code),
4051                method="POST",

4062                response_text=None,

Run an agent.

async def run_streaming_agent_and_wait( self, agent_id: str, body: AgentRunStreamRequest, *, timeout: float | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> seclai._generated.models.agent_run_response.AgentRunResponse: 
4067    async def run_streaming_agent_and_wait(
4068        self,
4069        agent_id: str,
4070        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
4071        *,
4072        timeout: float | None = None,
4073        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
4074    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
4075        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion (async).
4076
4077        This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE).
4078        The method returns when an `event: done` message is received.
4079        """
4080        import json as _json
4081        import time as _time
4082
4083        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
4084
4085        merged_headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
4086            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
4087        )
4088        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
4089
4090        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
4091        start = _time.monotonic()
4092
4093        try:
4094            async with self._client.stream(
4095                "POST",
4096                path,
4097                json=body,
4098                headers=merged_headers,
4099                timeout=timeout_seconds,
4100            ) as response:
4101                if response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY:
4102                    payload = response.json()
4103                    raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
4104                        message=f"Request failed with status {response.status_code}",
4105                        status_code=int(response.status_code),
4106                        method="POST",
4107                        url=self._build_url(path),
4108                        response_text=response.text,
4109                        validation_error=HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload),
4110                    )
4111                _raise_for_status(response)
4112
4113                current_event: str | None = None
4114                data_lines: list[str] = []
4115                last_seen: AgentRunResponse | None = None
4116
4117                def dispatch() -> AgentRunResponse | None:
4118                    nonlocal current_event, data_lines, last_seen
4119                    if current_event is None and not data_lines:
4120                        return None
4121                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
4122                    evt = current_event
4123                    current_event = None
4124                    data_lines = []
4125                    if not data:
4126                        return None
4127                    if evt not in {"init", "done"}:
4128                        return None
4129                    try:
4130                        parsed_dict = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
4131                        parsed = AgentRunResponse.from_dict(parsed_dict)
4132                        last_seen = parsed
4133                        if evt == "done":
4134                            return parsed
4135                    except Exception:
4136                        return None
4137                    return None
4138
4139                async for line in response.aiter_lines():
4140                    if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
4141                        raise SeclaiError(
4142                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
4143                        )
4144
4145                    if line == "":
4146                        done = dispatch()
4147                        if done is not None:
4148                            return done
4149                        continue
4150                    if line.startswith(":"):
4151                        continue
4152                    if line.startswith("event:"):
4153                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
4154                        continue
4155                    if line.startswith("data:"):
4156                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
4157                        continue
4158
4159                done = dispatch()
4160                if done is not None:
4161                    return done
4162                if last_seen is not None:
4163                    return last_seen
4164                raise SeclaiError("Stream ended before receiving a 'done' event.")
4165        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
4166            raise SeclaiError(
4167                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
4168            ) from e

Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion (async).

This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE). The method returns when an event: done message is received.

4236    async def get_agent_run(

4277            response=response,
4278            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
4279        )

4281            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
4282                message="Empty response",

4285                url=self._build_url(path),

4369        source_connection_content_version: str,
4370        *,
4371        start: int = 0,
4372        end: int = 5000,
4373    ) -> ContentDetailResponse:
4374        """Get content detail.
4375
4376        Fetches a slice of a content version (use `start`/`end` to page through large content).
4377
4378        Args:
4379            source_connection_content_version: Content version identifier.
4380            start: Start offset for returned content detail.
4381            end: End offset for returned content detail.

4592        """Upload a file to a specific source connection.
4593
4594        Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.
4595
4596        Maximum file size: 200 MiB.
4597
4598        Supported MIME types:
4599            - `application/epub+zip`
4600            - `application/json`
4601            - `application/msword`
4602            - `application/pdf`
4603            - `application/vnd.ms-excel`
4604            - `application/vnd.ms-outlook`
4605            - `application/vnd.ms-powerpoint`
4606            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation`
4607            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`
4608            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`
4609            - `application/xml`
4610            - `application/zip`
4611            - `audio/flac`
4612            - `audio/mp4`
4613            - `audio/mpeg`
4614            - `audio/ogg`
4615            - `audio/wav`
4616            - `image/bmp`
4617            - `image/gif`
4618            - `image/jpeg`
4619            - `image/png`
4620            - `image/tiff`
4621            - `image/webp`
4622            - `text/csv`
4623            - `text/html`
4624            - `text/markdown` / `text/x-markdown`
4625            - `text/plain`
4626            - `text/xml`
4627            - `video/mp4`
4628            - `video/quicktime`
4629            - `video/x-msvideo`
4630
4631        If `mime_type` is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it.
4632        If the upload is sent as `application/octet-stream`, the server attempts to infer
4633        the type from the file extension.
4634
4635        Notes:
4636            - If `file` is a path or `bytes`, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and
4637              closes it before returning.
4638            - If `file` is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.
4639
4640        Args:
4641            source_connection_id: Source connection identifier.
4642            file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like.
4643            title: Optional title for the uploaded file.
4644            metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content.
4645            file_name: Optional filename to send when `file` is bytes or a file-like.
4646            mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.
4647
4648        Returns:
4649            Upload response details.

4651        Raises:
4652            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
4653            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

4658            BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost,
4659        )

4661
4662        created_payload: BinaryIO | None = None
4663        try:
4664            upload_file: File
4665            if isinstance(file, File):
4666                upload_file = file
4667            elif isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
4668                file_path = Path(file)
4669                resolved_file_name = file_name or file_path.name
4670                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_file_name)
4671                created_payload = file_path.open("rb")
4672                upload_file = File(
4673                    payload=created_payload,
4674                    file_name=resolved_file_name,
4675                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4676                )
4677            elif isinstance(file, bytes):
4678                created_payload = BytesIO(file)
4679                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
4680                upload_file = File(
4681                    payload=created_payload,
4682                    file_name=file_name,
4683                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4684                )
4685            else:
4686                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
4687                upload_file = File(
4688                    payload=file,
4689                    file_name=file_name,
4690                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
4691                )
4692
4693            body = BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost(
4694                file=upload_file,
4695                title=UNSET if title is None else title,
4696            )
4697
4698            if metadata is not None:
4699                body["metadata"] = _json.dumps(metadata)
4700
4701            endpoint_path = f"/sources/{source_connection_id}/upload"
4702
4703            http = (await self._async_generated_client()).get_async_httpx_client()
4704            raw = await http.request(
4705                "POST",
4706                endpoint_path,
4707                files=body.to_multipart(),
4708            )
4709

4711            if raw.status_code == 200:
4712                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
4713                parsed = FileUploadResponse.from_dict(payload)
4714            elif raw.status_code == 422:
4715                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
4716                parsed = HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload)

4721                headers=raw.headers,
4722                parsed=parsed,

Upload a file to a specific source connection.

Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.

Maximum file size: 200 MiB.

Supported MIME types: - application/epub+zip - application/json - application/msword - application/pdf - application/vnd.ms-excel - application/vnd.ms-outlook - application/vnd.ms-powerpoint - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document - application/xml - application/zip - audio/flac - audio/mp4 - audio/mpeg - audio/ogg - audio/wav - image/bmp - image/gif - image/jpeg - image/png - image/tiff - image/webp - text/csv - text/html - text/markdown / text/x-markdown - text/plain - text/xml - video/mp4 - video/quicktime - video/x-msvideo

If mime_type is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it. If the upload is sent as application/octet-stream, the server attempts to infer the type from the file extension.

Notes: - If file is a path or bytes, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and closes it before returning. - If file is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.

Args: source_connection_id: Source connection identifier. file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like. title: Optional title for the uploaded file. metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content. file_name: Optional filename to send when file is bytes or a file-like. mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.

Returns: Upload response details.

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
