Args: agent_id: Agent identifier.

Returns: Agent details.

async def update_agent( self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
4917    async def update_agent(self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
4918        """Update an agent.
4919
4920        Args:
4921            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4922            body: Fields to update.
4923
4924        Returns:
4925            The updated agent.
4926        """
4927        return cast(
4928            dict[str, Any], await self.request("PUT", f"/agents/{agent_id}", json=body)
4929        )

Update an agent.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Fields to update.

Returns: The updated agent.

async def delete_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> None: 
4931    async def delete_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> None:
4932        """Delete an agent.
4933
4934        Args:
4935            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4936        """
4937        await self.request("DELETE", f"/agents/{agent_id}")

Delete an agent.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier.

async def export_agent(self, agent_id: str, *, download: bool = True) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
4941    async def export_agent(
4942        self, agent_id: str, *, download: bool = True
4943    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
4944        """Export an agent definition as a portable JSON snapshot.
4945
4946        Args:
4947            agent_id: Agent identifier.
4948            download: When True (default), the server sets Content-Disposition: attachment.
4949
4950        Returns:
4951            The exported agent snapshot.
4952        """
4953        params = _strip_none({"download": download})
4954        return cast(
4955            dict[str, Any],
4956            await self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/export", params=params),
4957        )

Export an agent definition as a portable JSON snapshot.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. download: When True (default), the server sets Content-Disposition: attachment.

Returns: The exported agent snapshot.

async def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
4959    async def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
4960        """Validate an ``agent_definition`` payload without creating an agent.
4961
4962        Use before :meth:`create_agent` or :meth:`update_agent` with an
4963        ``agent_definition`` to surface ``unresolved_refs`` — workflow
4964        references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or
4965        sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned
4966        ids back in ``entity_remap`` on the commit call to substitute them.
4967
4968        Args:
4969            body: ``{"agent_definition": ...}`` — same shape produced by
4970                :meth:`export_agent`.
4971
4972        Returns:
4973            Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any
4974            ``unresolved_refs``.
4975
4976        Raises:
4977            SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including
4978                HTTP 422 when the ``agent_definition`` fails validation.
4979                On 422 the response body (available as
4980                ``SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text``) is an
4981                ``AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse`` listing each field
4982                error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical
4983                ``source`` echo.
4984        """
4985        return cast(
4986            dict[str, Any],
4987            await self.request("POST", "/agents/preview-import", json=body),
4988        )

Validate an agent_definition payload without creating an agent.

Use before create_agent() or update_agent() with an agent_definition to surface unresolved_refs — workflow references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned ids back in entity_remap on the commit call to substitute them.

Args: body: {"agent_definition": ...} — same shape produced by export_agent().

Returns: Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any unresolved_refs.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including HTTP 422 when the agent_definition fails validation. On 422 the response body (available as SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text) is an AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse listing each field error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical source echo.

async def get_agent_definition(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]:

4997
4998        Returns:
4999            The agent's step definition.
5000        """
5001        return cast(
5002            dict[str, Any], await self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition")
5003        )

Get the step definition for an agent.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier.

Returns: The agent's step definition.

5008        """Update the step definition for an agent.

5012            body: New definition payload (must include ``change_id`` for optimistic locking).
5013
5014        Returns:
5015            The updated agent definition.
5016        """
5017        return cast(
5018            dict[str, Any],
5019            await self.request(
5020                "PUT",
5021                f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition",
5022                json=body,
5023            ),
5024        )

Update the step definition for an agent.

Search agent runs across agents.

Cancel an in-progress agent run.

Args: run_id: Run identifier.

async def upload_agent_input( self, agent_id: str, *, file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | typing.BinaryIO, file_name: str | None = None, mime_type: str | None = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
5056    async def upload_agent_input(
5057        self,
5058        agent_id: str,
5059        *,
5060        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
5061        file_name: str | None = None,
5062        mime_type: str | None = None,
5063    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5064        """Upload a file as input for an agent run.
5065
5066        Args:
5067            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5068            file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like).
5069            file_name: Filename to send.
5070            mime_type: MIME type of the file.
5071
5072        Returns:
5073            Upload response with the input upload ID.
5074        """
5075        payload, created = Seclai._prepare_upload_payload(
5076            self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]  # reuse sync helper from async context
5077            file,
5078            file_name,
5079            mime_type,
5080        )
5081        try:
5082            response = await self._client.post(
5083                f"/agents/{agent_id}/upload-input",
5084                files={"file": payload},
5085                headers=await _merge_request_headers_async(
5086                    options=self._options, request_headers=None
5087                ),
5088            )
5089            _raise_for_status(response)
5090            return cast(dict[str, Any], response.json())
5091        finally:
5092            if created is not None:
5093                created.close()

Upload a file as input for an agent run.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like). file_name: Filename to send. mime_type: MIME type of the file.

Returns: Upload response with the input upload ID.

async def get_agent_input_upload_status(self, agent_id: str, upload_id: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
5095    async def get_agent_input_upload_status(
5096        self, agent_id: str, upload_id: str
5097    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
5098        """Get the status of an agent input upload.
5099
5100        Args:
5101            agent_id: Agent identifier.
5102            upload_id: Upload identifier.
5103
5104        Returns:
5105            Upload status details.
5106        """
5107        return cast(
5108            dict[str, Any],
5109            await self.request(
5110                "GET",
5111                f"/agents/{agent_id}/input-uploads/{upload_id}",
5112            ),
5113        )

Get the status of an agent input upload.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. upload_id: Upload identifier.

Returns: Upload status details.

async def download_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> httpx.Response: 
5956    async def download_source_export(
5957        self, source_id: str, export_id: str
5958    ) -> httpx.Response:
5959        """Download a completed source export.
5960
5961        Returns a **streaming** response. The caller is responsible for
5962        consuming and closing the response::
5963
5964            response = await client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
5965            async with response:
5966                async for chunk in response.aiter_bytes():
5967                    f.write(chunk)
5968
5969        Args:
5970            source_id: Source identifier.
5971            export_id: Export identifier.
5972
5973        Returns:
5974            A streaming ``httpx.Response``. Must be closed by the caller.
5975        """
5976        headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
5977            options=self._options, request_headers=None
5978        )
5979        request = self._client.build_request(
5980            "GET",
5981            f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/download",
5982            headers=headers,
5983        )
5984        response = await self._client.send(request, stream=True)
5985        _raise_for_status(response)
5986        return response

Download a completed source export.

Returns a streaming response. The caller is responsible for consuming and closing the response::

response = await client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
async with response:
    async for chunk in response.aiter_bytes():
        f.write(chunk)

Args: source_id: Source identifier. export_id: Export identifier.

Returns: A streaming httpx.Response. Must be closed by the caller.

5999        """
6000        return cast(
6001            dict[str, Any],
6002            await self.request(
6003                "POST",
6004                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/estimate",
6005                json=body,
6006            ),
6007        )

Estimate the size/cost of a source export.

Args: source_id: Source identifier. body: Export estimation parameters.

Returns: Export estimate.

async def list_solutions( self, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50, sort: str = 'created_at', order: str = 'desc') -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
6091    async def list_solutions(
6092        self,
6093        *,
6094        page: int = 1,
6095        limit: int = 50,
6096        sort: str = "created_at",
6097        order: str = "desc",
6098    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
6099        """List solutions.
6100
6101        Args:
6102            page: Page number (1-indexed).
6103            limit: Items per page.

6105            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).

6107        Returns:
6108            Paginated list of solutions.
6109        """
6110        return cast(
6111            dict[str, Any],
6112            await self.request(
6113                "GET",
6114                "/solutions",
6115                params=_strip_none(
6116                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
6117                ),
6118            ),
6119        )

6543            ),
6544        )

async def paginate( self, method: str, path: str, *, params: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, limit: int = 50, items_key: str = 'data') -> AsyncGenerator[dict[str, typing.Any]]: 
7045    async def paginate(
7046        self,
7047        method: str,
7048        path: str,
7049        *,
7050        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
7051        limit: int = 50,
7052        items_key: str = "data",
7053    ) -> AsyncGenerator[dict[str, Any]]:
7054        """Auto-paginate a list endpoint, yielding items lazily.
7055
7056        Fetches pages sequentially until a page returns fewer items than ``limit``
7057        or the items list is empty.  Use ``async for item in client.paginate(...):``
7058        to iterate.
7059
7060        Args:
7061            method: HTTP method (typically ``"GET"``).
7062            path: API path.
7063            params: Extra query parameters (``page`` and ``limit`` are managed automatically).
7064            limit: Items per page.
7065            items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default ``"data"``).
7066
7067        Yields:
7068            Individual items from each page.
7069        """
7070        page = 1
7071        base_params = dict(params) if params else {}
7072        while True:
7073            base_params["page"] = page
7074            base_params["limit"] = limit
7075            result = await self.request(method, path, params=base_params)
7076            if not isinstance(result, dict):
7077                break
7078            items = result.get(items_key, [])
7079            if not isinstance(items, list):
7080                break
7081            for item in items:
7082                yield cast(dict[str, Any], item)
7083            if len(items) < limit:
7084                break
7085            page += 1

Auto-paginate a list endpoint, yielding items lazily.

Fetches pages sequentially until a page returns fewer items than limit or the items list is empty. Use async for item in client.paginate(...): to iterate.

Args: method: HTTP method (typically "GET"). path: API path. params: Extra query parameters (page and limit are managed automatically). limit: Items per page. items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default "data").

Yields: Individual items from each page.

async def run_agent_and_poll( self, agent_id: str, body: seclai._generated.models.agent_run_request.AgentRunRequest, *, poll_interval: float = 2.0, include_step_outputs: bool = False) -> seclai._generated.models.agent_run_response.AgentRunResponse: 
7089    async def run_agent_and_poll(
7090        self,
7091        agent_id: str,
7092        body: AgentRunRequest,
7093        *,
7094        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
7095        include_step_outputs: bool = False,
7096    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
7097        """Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.
7098
7099        Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls ``get_agent_run`` until
7100        the status is ``"completed"`` or ``"failed"``.
7101
7102        Args:
7103            agent_id: Agent identifier.
7104            body: Agent run request payload.
7105            poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0).
7106            include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.
7107
7108        Returns:
7109            The final agent run (completed or failed).
7110
7111        Raises:
7112            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
7113            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
7114        """
7115        import asyncio
7116
7117        run = await self.run_agent(agent_id, body)
7118        while run.status not in ("completed", "failed"):
7119            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
7120            run = await self.get_agent_run(
7121                run.run_id, include_step_outputs=include_step_outputs
7122            )
7123        return run

Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.

Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls get_agent_run until the status is "completed" or "failed".

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Agent run request payload. poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0). include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.

Returns: The final agent run (completed or failed).

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

async def run_streaming_agent( self, agent_id: str, body: AgentRunStreamRequest, *, timeout: float | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> AsyncGenerator[tuple[str, dict[str, typing.Any]]]: 
7125    async def run_streaming_agent(
7126        self,
7127        agent_id: str,
7128        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
7129        *,
7130        timeout: float | None = None,
7131        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
7132    ) -> AsyncGenerator[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]:
7133        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and yield events as they arrive.
7134
7135        Use ``async for event_type, data in client.run_streaming_agent(...)`` to
7136        process events lazily without buffering the entire stream.
7137
7138        Args:
7139            agent_id: Agent identifier.
7140            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
7141            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout.
7142            headers: Optional extra headers.
7143
7144        Yields:
7145            ``(event_type, parsed_data)`` tuples for each SSE event.
7146
7147        Raises:
7148            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
7149            SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received.
7150            SeclaiError: If the stream times out.
7151        """
7152        import json as _json
7153
7154        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
7155        merged_headers = await _merge_request_headers_async(
7156            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
7157        )
7158        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
7159        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
7160        start = time.monotonic()
7161
7162        try:
7163            async with self._client.stream(
7164                "POST",
7165                path,
7166                json=body,
7167                headers=merged_headers,
7168                timeout=timeout_seconds,
7169            ) as response:
7170                _raise_for_status(response)
7171                current_event: str | None = None
7172                data_lines: list[str] = []
7173
7174                async for line in response.aiter_lines():
7175                    if time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
7176                        raise SeclaiError(
7177                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
7178                        )
7179                    if line == "":
7180                        if current_event and data_lines:
7181                            data = "\n".join(data_lines)
7182                            try:
7183                                parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
7184                            except Exception:
7185                                parsed = {"raw": data}
7186                            if current_event == "error":
7187                                raise SeclaiStreamingError(
7188                                    parsed.get("message", data),
7189                                    run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
7190                                )
7191                            yield (current_event, parsed)
7192                        current_event = None
7193                        data_lines = []
7194                        continue
7195                    if line.startswith(":"):
7196                        continue
7197                    if line.startswith("event:"):
7198                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
7199                        continue
7200                    if line.startswith("data:"):
7201                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
7202                        continue
7203
7204                if current_event and data_lines:
7205                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
7206                    try:
7207                        parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
7208                    except Exception:
7209                        parsed = {"raw": data}
7210                    if current_event == "error":
7211                        raise SeclaiStreamingError(
7212                            parsed.get("message", data),
7213                            run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
7214                        )
7215                    yield (current_event, parsed)
7216        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
7217            raise SeclaiError(
7218                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
7219            ) from e

Run an agent via SSE streaming and yield events as they arrive.

Use async for event_type, data in client.run_streaming_agent(...) to process events lazily without buffering the entire stream.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Streaming agent run request payload. timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout. headers: Optional extra headers.

Yields: (event_type, parsed_data) tuples for each SSE event.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code. SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received. SeclaiError: If the stream times out.

DEFAULT_SSO_REGION = 'us-west-2'

class Seclai(seclai.seclai._SeclaiBase): 
 534class Seclai(_SeclaiBase):
 535    """Seclai synchronous client.
 536
 537    Most users should prefer the typed convenience methods (e.g. `run_agent`, `list_sources`)
 538    which raise SDK exceptions on failures.
 539
 540    This client can be used as a context manager:
 541
 542        with Seclai(api_key="...") as client:
 543            sources = client.list_sources()
 544    """
 545
 546    def __init__(
 547        self,
 548        *,
 549        api_key: str | None = None,
 550        access_token: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
 551        timeout: float = 30.0,
 552        api_key_header: str = "x-api-key",
 553        default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
 554        http_client: httpx.Client | None = None,
 555        profile: str | None = None,
 556        config_dir: str | None = None,
 557        auto_refresh: bool = True,
 558        account_id: str | None = None,
 559    ) -> None:
 560        """Create a synchronous Seclai client.
 561
 562        Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):
 563
 564        1. Explicit ``api_key``
 565        2. Explicit ``access_token`` (static string or provider callable)
 566        3. ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` environment variable
 567        4. SSO profile from ``~/.seclai/config`` + cached tokens
 568
 569        Args:
 570            api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` is used.
 571            access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one.
 572                Mutually exclusive with ``api_key``.
 573            timeout: Request timeout (seconds).
 574            api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key.
 575            default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request.
 576            http_client: Optional pre-configured ``httpx.Client`` to use.
 577            profile: SSO profile name from ``~/.seclai/config``.
 578            config_dir: Override the config directory path.
 579            auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to ``True``.
 580            account_id: Target organization account ID (``X-Account-Id`` header).
 581
 582        Raises:
 583            SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.
 584        """
 585        super().__init__(
 586            api_key=api_key,
 587            access_token=access_token,
 588            timeout=timeout,
 589            api_key_header=api_key_header,
 590            default_headers=default_headers,
 591            profile=profile,
 592            config_dir=config_dir,
 593            auto_refresh=auto_refresh,
 594            account_id=account_id,
 595        )
 596        self._client = http_client or httpx.Client(
 597            base_url=SECLAI_API_URL,
 598            timeout=self._options.timeout,
 599            headers=self._default_headers(),
 600        )
 601        self._owns_client = http_client is None
 602
 603    def close(self) -> None:
 604        """Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.
 605
 606        If you created the client with a custom ``http_client``, this is a no-op
 607        for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).
 608        """
 609        if self._owns_client:
 610            self._client.close()
 611        if self._owns_generated_client and self._generated_client_instance is not None:
 612            self._generated_client_instance.get_httpx_client().close()
 613
 614    def __enter__(self) -> Self:
 615        """Enter a context manager and return self."""
 616        return self
 617
 618    def __exit__(self, exc_type: object, exc: object, tb: object) -> None:
 619        """Exit the context manager and close resources.
 620
 621        Args:
 622            exc_type: Exception type (if any).
 623            exc: Exception instance (if any).
 624            tb: Traceback (if any).
 625        """
 626        self.close()
 627
 628    def request(
 629        self,
 630        method: str,
 631        path: str,
 632        *,
 633        params: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
 634        json: JSONValue | None = None,
 635        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
 636    ) -> JSONValue | str | None:
 637        """Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.
 638
 639        This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed
 640        convenience methods on this client.
 641
 642        Args:
 643            method: HTTP method (e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`).
 644            path: Request path relative to `SECLAI_API_URL` (e.g. `"/sources/"`).
 645            params: Query parameters.
 646            json: JSON body to send.
 647            headers: Per-request header overrides.
 648
 649        Returns:
 650            Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or `None` for empty bodies.
 651
 652        Raises:
 653            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.
 654        """
 655        response = self._client.request(
 656            method,
 657            path,
 658            params=params,
 659            json=json,
 660            headers=_merge_request_headers(
 661                options=self._options, request_headers=headers
 662            ),
 663        )
 664        _raise_for_status(response)
 665        if not response.content:
 666            return None
 667        content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
 668        if "application/json" in content_type:
 669            return cast(JSONValue, response.json())
 670        return response.text

 689            sync_detailed,

 694            agent_id=agent_id, client=self._sync_generated_client(), body=body
 695        )
 696        self._raise_for_openapi_response(
 697            method="POST",
 698            path=path,
 699            response=response,
 700            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
 701        )
 702        if response.parsed is None:
 703            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
 704                message="Empty response",
 705                status_code=int(response.status_code),
 706                method="POST",
 707                url=self._build_url(path),
 708                response_text=None,
 709            )
 710        parsed = response.parsed
 711        if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
 712            raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
 713                message="Validation error",
 714                status_code=int(response.status_code),
 715                method="POST",
 716                url=self._build_url(path),
 717                response_text=None,
 718                validation_error=parsed,
 719            )
 720        return parsed

 722    def run_streaming_agent_and_wait(
 723        self,
 724        agent_id: str,
 725        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
 726        *,
 727        timeout: float | None = None,
 728        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
 729    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
 730        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion.
 731
 732        This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE).
 733        The method returns when an `event: done` message is received.
 734
 735        Args:
 736            agent_id: Agent identifier.
 737            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
 738            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait for the final result (client-side). Defaults to this
 739                client's configured timeout.
 740            headers: Optional extra headers for this request.
 741
 742        Returns:
 743            The final agent run payload from the `done` event.
 744
 745        Raises:
 746            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
 747            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
 748            SeclaiError: If the stream ends early or times out.
 749        """
 750        import json as _json
 751        import time as _time
 752
 753        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
 754
 755        merged_headers = _merge_request_headers(
 756            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
 757        )
 758        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
 759
 760        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
 761        start = _time.monotonic()
 762
 763        try:
 764            with self._client.stream(
 765                "POST",
 766                path,
 767                json=body,
 768                headers=merged_headers,
 769                timeout=timeout_seconds,
 770            ) as response:
 771                if response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY:
 772                    payload = response.json()
 773                    raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
 774                        message=f"Request failed with status {response.status_code}",
 775                        status_code=int(response.status_code),
 776                        method="POST",
 777                        url=self._build_url(path),
 778                        response_text=response.text,
 779                        validation_error=HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload),
 780                    )
 781                _raise_for_status(response)
 782
 783                current_event: str | None = None
 784                data_lines: list[str] = []
 785                last_seen: AgentRunResponse | None = None
 786
 787                def dispatch() -> AgentRunResponse | None:
 788                    nonlocal current_event, data_lines, last_seen
 789                    if current_event is None and not data_lines:
 790                        return None
 791                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
 792                    evt = current_event
 793                    current_event = None
 794                    data_lines = []
 795                    if not data:
 796                        return None
 797                    if evt not in {"init", "done"}:
 798                        return None
 799                    try:
 800                        parsed_dict = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
 801                        parsed = AgentRunResponse.from_dict(parsed_dict)
 802                        last_seen = parsed
 803                        if evt == "done":
 804                            return parsed
 805                    except Exception:
 806                        return None
 807                    return None
 808
 809                for line in response.iter_lines():
 810                    if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
 811                        raise SeclaiError(
 812                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
 813                        )
 814
 815                    if line == "":
 816                        done = dispatch()
 817                        if done is not None:
 818                            return done
 819                        continue
 820                    if line.startswith(":"):
 821                        continue
 822                    if line.startswith("event:"):
 823                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
 824                        continue
 825                    if line.startswith("data:"):
 826                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
 827                        continue
 828
 829                done = dispatch()
 830                if done is not None:
 831                    return done
 832                if last_seen is not None:
 833                    return last_seen
 834                raise SeclaiError("Stream ended before receiving a 'done' event.")
 835        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
 836            raise SeclaiError(
 837                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
 838            ) from e

1036        start: int = 0,
1037        end: int = 5000,
1038    ) -> ContentDetailResponse:
1039        """Get content detail.
1040
1041        Fetches a slice of a content version (use `start`/`end` to page through large content).
1042
1043        Args:
1044            source_connection_content_version: Content version identifier.
1045            start: Start offset for returned content detail.
1046            end: End offset for returned content detail.
1047
1048        Returns:
1049            Content details for the requested range.
1050
1051        Raises:
1052            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
1053            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
1054        """
1055        from seclai._generated.api.contents.get_content_detail_api_contents_source_connection_content_version_get import (
1056            sync_detailed,
1057        )
1058

1060        response = sync_detailed(
1061            source_connection_content_version=source_connection_content_version,
1062            client=self._sync_generated_client(),
1063            start=start,
1064            end=end,
1065        )
1066        self._raise_for_openapi_response(
1067            method="GET",
1068            path=path,
1069            response=response,
1070            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
1071        )
1072        if response.parsed is None:
1073            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
1074                message="Empty response",
1075                status_code=int(response.status_code),
1076                method="GET",
1077                url=self._build_url(path),
1078                response_text=None,
1079            )
1080        parsed = response.parsed
1081        if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
1082            raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
1083                message="Validation error",
1084                status_code=int(response.status_code),
1085                method="GET",
1086                url=self._build_url(path),
1087                response_text=None,
1088                validation_error=parsed,
1089            )
1090        return parsed
1091

1181    def list_sources(

1247    def upload_file_to_source(
1248        self,
1249        source_connection_id: str,
1250        *,
1251        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
1252        title: str | None = None,
1253        metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
1254        file_name: str | None = None,
1255        mime_type: str | None = None,
1256    ) -> FileUploadResponse:
1257        """Upload a file to a specific source connection.
1258
1259        Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.
1260
1261        Maximum file size: 200 MiB.
1262
1263        Supported MIME types:
1264            - `application/epub+zip`
1265            - `application/json`
1266            - `application/msword`
1267            - `application/pdf`
1268            - `application/vnd.ms-excel`
1269            - `application/vnd.ms-outlook`
1270            - `application/vnd.ms-powerpoint`
1271            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation`
1272            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`
1273            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`
1274            - `application/xml`
1275            - `application/zip`
1276            - `audio/flac`
1277            - `audio/mp4`
1278            - `audio/mpeg`
1279            - `audio/ogg`
1280            - `audio/wav`
1281            - `image/bmp`
1282            - `image/gif`
1283            - `image/jpeg`
1284            - `image/png`
1285            - `image/tiff`
1286            - `image/webp`
1287            - `text/csv`
1288            - `text/html`
1289            - `text/markdown` / `text/x-markdown`
1290            - `text/plain`
1291            - `text/xml`
1292            - `video/mp4`
1293            - `video/quicktime`
1294            - `video/x-msvideo`
1295
1296        If `mime_type` is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it.
1297        If the upload is sent as `application/octet-stream`, the server attempts to infer
1298        the type from the file extension.
1299
1300        Notes:
1301            - If `file` is a path or `bytes`, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and
1302              closes it before returning.
1303            - If `file` is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.
1304
1305        Args:
1306            source_connection_id: Source connection identifier.
1307            file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like.
1308            title: Optional title for the uploaded file.
1309            metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content.
1310            file_name: Optional filename to send when `file` is bytes or a file-like.
1311            mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.
1312
1313        Returns:
1314            Upload response details.
1315
1316        Raises:
1317            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
1318            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
1319        """
1320        import json as _json
1321
1322        from seclai._generated.models.body_upload_file_to_source_api_sources_source_connection_id_upload_post import (
1323            BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost,
1324        )
1325        from seclai._generated.types import UNSET, File
1326
1327        created_payload: BinaryIO | None = None
1328        try:
1329            upload_file: File
1330            if isinstance(file, File):
1331                upload_file = file
1332            elif isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
1333                file_path = Path(file)
1334                resolved_file_name = file_name or file_path.name
1335                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_file_name)
1336                created_payload = file_path.open("rb")
1337                upload_file = File(
1338                    payload=created_payload,
1339                    file_name=resolved_file_name,
1340                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1341                )
1342            elif isinstance(file, bytes):
1343                created_payload = BytesIO(file)
1344                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1345                upload_file = File(
1346                    payload=created_payload,
1347                    file_name=file_name,
1348                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1349                )
1350            else:
1351                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1352                upload_file = File(
1353                    payload=file,
1354                    file_name=file_name,
1355                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1356                )
1357
1358            body = BodyUploadFileToSourceApiSourcesSourceConnectionIdUploadPost(
1359                file=upload_file,
1360                title=UNSET if title is None else title,
1361            )
1362
1363            if metadata is not None:
1364                body["metadata"] = _json.dumps(metadata)
1365
1366            endpoint_path = f"/sources/{source_connection_id}/upload"
1367
1368            # Note: openapi-python-client currently struggles with Seclai's spec for this endpoint
1369            # due to duplicate schema names, so we send the multipart request directly and parse
1370            # into our SDK model types.
1371            http = self._sync_generated_client().get_httpx_client()
1372            raw = http.request(
1373                "POST",
1374                endpoint_path,
1375                files=body.to_multipart(),
1376            )
1377
1378            parsed: FileUploadResponse | HTTPValidationError | None = None
1379            if raw.status_code == 200:
1380                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
1381                parsed = FileUploadResponse.from_dict(payload)
1382            elif raw.status_code == 422:
1383                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
1384                parsed = HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload)
1385
1386            response = OpenAPIResponse(
1387                status_code=HTTPStatus(raw.status_code),
1388                content=raw.content,
1389                headers=raw.headers,
1390                parsed=parsed,
1391            )
1392            self._raise_for_openapi_response(
1393                method="POST",
1394                path=endpoint_path,
1395                response=response,
1396                ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
1397            )
1398            if response.parsed is None:
1399                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
1400                    message="Empty response",
1401                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
1402                    method="POST",
1403                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
1404                    response_text=None,
1405                )
1406            parsed_response = cast(
1407                FileUploadResponse | HTTPValidationError, response.parsed
1408            )
1409            if isinstance(parsed_response, HTTPValidationError):
1410                raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
1411                    message="Validation error",
1412                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
1413                    method="POST",
1414                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
1415                    response_text=None,
1416                    validation_error=parsed_response,
1417                )
1418            if not isinstance(parsed_response, FileUploadResponse):
1419                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
1420                    message="Unexpected response shape",
1421                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
1422                    method="POST",
1423                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
1424                    response_text=None,
1425                )
1426            return parsed_response
1427        finally:
1428            if created_payload is not None:
1429                created_payload.close()
1430
1431    def upload_file_to_content(
1432        self,
1433        source_connection_content_version: str,
1434        *,
1435        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
1436        title: str | None = None,
1437        metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
1438        file_name: str | None = None,
1439        mime_type: str | None = None,
1440    ) -> FileUploadResponse:
1441        """Replace an existing content version with a new file upload.
1442
1443        This uploads a new file to `/contents/{source_connection_content_version}/upload` and replaces
1444        the content backing that existing content version.
1445
1446        Args:
1447            source_connection_content_version: Source connection content version identifier.
1448            file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like.
1449            title: Optional title for the uploaded file.
1450            metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content.
1451            file_name: Optional filename to send when `file` is bytes or a file-like.
1452            mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.
1453
1454        Returns:
1455            Upload response details.
1456
1457        Raises:
1458            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
1459            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
1460        """
1461        import json as _json
1462
1463        from seclai._generated.api.contents.upload_file_to_content_api_contents_source_connection_content_version_upload_post import (
1464            sync_detailed,
1465        )
1466        from seclai._generated.models.body_upload_file_to_content_api_contents_source_connection_content_version_upload_post import (
1467            BodyUploadFileToContentApiContentsSourceConnectionContentVersionUploadPost,
1468        )
1469        from seclai._generated.types import UNSET, File
1470
1471        created_payload: BinaryIO | None = None
1472        try:
1473            upload_file: File
1474            if isinstance(file, File):
1475                upload_file = file
1476            elif isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
1477                file_path = Path(file)
1478                resolved_file_name = file_name or file_path.name
1479                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_file_name)
1480                created_payload = file_path.open("rb")
1481                upload_file = File(
1482                    payload=created_payload,
1483                    file_name=resolved_file_name,
1484                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1485                )
1486            elif isinstance(file, bytes):
1487                created_payload = BytesIO(file)
1488                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1489                upload_file = File(
1490                    payload=created_payload,
1491                    file_name=file_name,
1492                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1493                )
1494            else:
1495                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1496                upload_file = File(
1497                    payload=file,
1498                    file_name=file_name,
1499                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1500                )
1501
1502            body = BodyUploadFileToContentApiContentsSourceConnectionContentVersionUploadPost(
1503                file=upload_file,
1504                title=UNSET if title is None else title,
1505                metadata=UNSET if metadata is None else _json.dumps(metadata),
1506            )
1507            endpoint_path = f"/contents/{source_connection_content_version}/upload"
1508            response = sync_detailed(
1509                source_connection_content_version=source_connection_content_version,
1510                client=self._sync_generated_client(),
1511                body=body,
1512            )
1513            self._raise_for_openapi_response(
1514                method="POST",
1515                path=endpoint_path,
1516                response=response,
1517                ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
1518            )
1519            if response.parsed is None:
1520                raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
1521                    message="Empty response",
1522                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
1523                    method="POST",
1524                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
1525                    response_text=None,
1526                )
1527            parsed = response.parsed
1528            if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
1529                raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
1530                    message="Validation error",
1531                    status_code=int(response.status_code),
1532                    method="POST",
1533                    url=self._build_url(endpoint_path),
1534                    response_text=None,
1535                    validation_error=parsed,
1536                )
1537            return parsed
1538        finally:
1539            if created_payload is not None:
1540                created_payload.close()
1541
1542    # ── Agents ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1543
1544    def list_agents(self, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50) -> dict[str, Any]:
1545        """List all agents.
1546
1547        Args:
1548            page: Page number (1-indexed).
1549            limit: Items per page.
1550
1551        Returns:
1552            Paginated list of agents.
1553        """
1554        return cast(
1555            dict[str, Any],
1556            self.request(
1557                "GET",
1558                "/agents",
1559                params=_strip_none({"page": page, "limit": limit}),
1560            ),
1561        )
1562
1563    def create_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1564        """Create a new agent.
1565
1566        Args:
1567            body: Agent creation payload (name, description, etc.).
1568
1569        Returns:
1570            The created agent.
1571        """
1572        return cast(dict[str, Any], self.request("POST", "/agents", json=body))
1573
1574    def get_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
1575        """Get an agent by ID.
1576
1577        Args:
1578            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1579
1580        Returns:
1581            Agent details.
1582        """
1583        return cast(dict[str, Any], self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}"))
1584
1585    def update_agent(self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1586        """Update an agent.
1587
1588        Args:
1589            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1590            body: Fields to update.
1591
1592        Returns:
1593            The updated agent.
1594        """
1595        return cast(
1596            dict[str, Any], self.request("PUT", f"/agents/{agent_id}", json=body)
1597        )
1598
1599    def delete_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> None:
1600        """Delete an agent.
1601
1602        Args:
1603            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1604        """
1605        self.request("DELETE", f"/agents/{agent_id}")
1606
1607    # ── Agent Export ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1608
1609    def export_agent(self, agent_id: str, *, download: bool = True) -> dict[str, Any]:
1610        """Export an agent definition as a portable JSON snapshot.
1611
1612        Args:
1613            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1614            download: When True (default), the server sets Content-Disposition: attachment.
1615
1616        Returns:
1617            The exported agent snapshot.
1618        """
1619        params = _strip_none({"download": download})
1620        return cast(
1621            dict[str, Any],
1622            self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/export", params=params),
1623        )
1624
1625    def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1626        """Validate an ``agent_definition`` payload without creating an agent.
1627
1628        Use before :meth:`create_agent` or :meth:`update_agent` with an
1629        ``agent_definition`` to surface ``unresolved_refs`` — workflow
1630        references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or
1631        sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned
1632        ids back in ``entity_remap`` on the commit call to substitute them.
1633
1634        Args:
1635            body: ``{"agent_definition": ...}`` — same shape produced by
1636                :meth:`export_agent`.
1637
1638        Returns:
1639            Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any
1640            ``unresolved_refs``.
1641
1642        Raises:
1643            SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including
1644                HTTP 422 when the ``agent_definition`` fails validation.
1645                On 422 the response body (available as
1646                ``SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text``) is an
1647                ``AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse`` listing each field
1648                error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical
1649                ``source`` echo.
1650        """
1651        return cast(
1652            dict[str, Any],
1653            self.request("POST", "/agents/preview-import", json=body),
1654        )
1655
1656    # ── Agent Definitions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1657
1658    def get_agent_definition(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
1659        """Get the step definition for an agent.
1660
1661        Args:
1662            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1663
1664        Returns:
1665            The agent's step definition.
1666        """
1667        return cast(
1668            dict[str, Any], self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition")
1669        )
1670
1671    def update_agent_definition(
1672        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
1673    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1674        """Update the step definition for an agent.
1675
1676        Args:
1677            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1678            body: New definition payload (must include ``change_id`` for optimistic locking).
1679
1680        Returns:
1681            The updated agent definition.
1682        """
1683        return cast(
1684            dict[str, Any],
1685            self.request(
1686                "PUT",
1687                f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition",
1688                json=body,
1689            ),
1690        )
1691
1692    # ── Agent Runs (additional) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
1693
1694    def search_agent_runs(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1695        """Search agent runs across agents.
1696
1697        Args:
1698            body: Search request with query filters.
1699
1700        Returns:
1701            Search results with matching runs.
1702        """
1703        return cast(
1704            dict[str, Any], self.request("POST", "/agents/runs/search", json=body)
1705        )
1706
1707    def cancel_agent_run(self, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
1708        """Cancel an in-progress agent run.
1709
1710        Args:
1711            run_id: Run identifier.
1712
1713        Returns:
1714            The updated agent run.
1715        """
1716        return cast(
1717            dict[str, Any], self.request("POST", f"/agents/runs/{run_id}/cancel")
1718        )
1719
1720    # ── Agent Input Uploads ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
1721
1722    def upload_agent_input(
1723        self,
1724        agent_id: str,
1725        *,
1726        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
1727        file_name: str | None = None,
1728        mime_type: str | None = None,
1729    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1730        """Upload a file as input for an agent run.
1731
1732        Args:
1733            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1734            file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like).
1735            file_name: Filename to send.
1736            mime_type: MIME type of the file.
1737
1738        Returns:
1739            Upload response with the input upload ID.
1740        """
1741        payload, created = self._prepare_upload_payload(file, file_name, mime_type)
1742        try:
1743            response = self._client.post(
1744                f"/agents/{agent_id}/upload-input",
1745                files={"file": payload},
1746                headers=_merge_request_headers(
1747                    options=self._options, request_headers=None
1748                ),
1749            )
1750            _raise_for_status(response)
1751            return cast(dict[str, Any], response.json())
1752        finally:
1753            if created is not None:
1754                created.close()
1755
1756    def get_agent_input_upload_status(
1757        self, agent_id: str, upload_id: str
1758    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1759        """Get the status of an agent input upload.
1760
1761        Args:
1762            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1763            upload_id: Upload identifier.
1764
1765        Returns:
1766            Upload status details.
1767        """
1768        return cast(
1769            dict[str, Any],
1770            self.request(
1771                "GET",
1772                f"/agents/{agent_id}/input-uploads/{upload_id}",
1773            ),
1774        )
1775
1776    # ── Agent AI Assistant ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1777
1778    def generate_agent_steps(
1779        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
1780    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1781        """Use the AI assistant to generate agent steps.
1782
1783        Args:
1784            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1785            body: Prompt and context for step generation.
1786
1787        Returns:
1788            Generated steps suggestion.
1789        """
1790        return cast(
1791            dict[str, Any],
1792            self.request(
1793                "POST",
1794                f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/generate-steps",
1795                json=body,
1796            ),
1797        )
1798
1799    def generate_step_config(
1800        self, agent_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
1801    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

1803

1805            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1806            body: Step type and context for config generation.
1807

1809            Generated step configuration.

1811        return cast(
1812            dict[str, Any],
1813            self.request(
1814                "POST",
1815                f"/agents/{agent_id}/ai-assistant/step-config",
1816                json=body,
1817            ),
1818        )
1819
1820    def get_agent_ai_conversation_history(self, agent_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:

1824            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1825
1826        Returns:
1827            Conversation history with the AI assistant.

1834            ),
1835        )
1836
1837    def mark_agent_ai_suggestion(
1838        self, agent_id: str, conversation_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
1839    ) -> None:

1841
1842        Args:

1847        self.request(
1848            "PATCH",

1850            json=body,
1851        )

2132    # ── Knowledge Bases ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2134    def list_knowledge_bases(
2135        self,
2136        *,
2137        page: int = 1,
2138        limit: int = 50,
2139        sort: str = "created_at",
2140        order: str = "desc",
2141    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

2143
2144        Args:
2145            page: Page number (1-indexed).
2146            limit: Items per page.
2147            sort: Sort field.
2148            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).
2149
2150        Returns:
2151            Paginated list of knowledge bases.
2152        """
2153        return cast(
2154            dict[str, Any],
2155            self.request(
2156                "GET",
2157                "/knowledge_bases",
2158                params=_strip_none(
2159                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
2160                ),
2161            ),
2162        )
2163
2164    def create_knowledge_base(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
2165        """Create a knowledge base.
2166

2168            body: Knowledge base creation payload.
2169

2171            The created knowledge base.
2172        """

2180

2222
2223    def list_memory_banks(

2234            page: Page number (1-indexed).

2239        Returns:

2445    # ── Sources (additional) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
2446
2447    def create_source(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
2448        """Create a new source.
2449
2450        Args:
2451            body: Source creation payload.
2452
2453        Returns:

2460

2462            source_id: Source identifier.

2465            Source details.

2470        """Update a source.
2471
2472        Args:
2473            source_id: Source identifier.

2488    def delete_source(self, source_id: str) -> None:
2489        """Delete a source.
2490
2491        Args:
2492            source_id: Source identifier.
2493        """
2494        self.request("DELETE", f"/sources/{source_id}")
2495
2496    def upload_inline_text_to_source(
2497        self, source_connection_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2498    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2499        """Upload inline text content to a source connection.
2500
2501        Args:
2502            source_connection_id: Source connection identifier.
2503            body: Inline text payload with title and content.
2504
2505        Returns:
2506            Upload response details.
2507        """
2508        return cast(
2509            dict[str, Any],
2510            self.request(
2511                "POST",
2512                f"/sources/{source_connection_id}",
2513                json=body,
2514            ),
2515        )
2516
2517    # ── Source Exports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2518
2519    def list_source_exports(
2520        self, source_id: str, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50
2521    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2522        """List exports for a source.
2523
2524        Args:
2525            source_id: Source identifier.
2526            page: Page number (1-indexed).
2527            limit: Items per page.
2528
2529        Returns:
2530            Paginated list of exports.
2531        """
2532        return cast(
2533            dict[str, Any],
2534            self.request(
2535                "GET",
2536                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports",
2537                params=_strip_none({"page": page, "limit": limit}),
2538            ),
2539        )
2540
2541    def create_source_export(
2542        self, source_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2543    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2544        """Create an export for a source.
2545
2546        Args:
2547            source_id: Source identifier.
2548            body: Export configuration (format, filters, etc.).
2549
2550        Returns:
2551            The created export.
2552        """
2553        return cast(
2554            dict[str, Any],
2555            self.request(
2556                "POST",
2557                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports",
2558                json=body,
2559            ),
2560        )
2561
2562    def get_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
2563        """Get details of a source export.
2564
2565        Args:
2566            source_id: Source identifier.
2567            export_id: Export identifier.
2568
2569        Returns:
2570            Export details.
2571        """
2572        return cast(
2573            dict[str, Any],
2574            self.request(
2575                "GET",
2576                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}",
2577            ),
2578        )
2579
2580    def cancel_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
2581        """Cancel a source export.
2582
2583        Args:
2584            source_id: Source identifier.
2585            export_id: Export identifier.
2586
2587        Returns:
2588            The updated export.
2589        """
2590        return cast(
2591            dict[str, Any],
2592            self.request(
2593                "POST",
2594                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/cancel",
2595            ),
2596        )
2597
2598    def delete_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> None:
2599        """Delete a source export.
2600
2601        Args:
2602            source_id: Source identifier.
2603            export_id: Export identifier.
2604        """
2605        self.request("DELETE", f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}")
2606
2607    def download_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> httpx.Response:
2608        """Download a completed source export.
2609
2610        Returns a **streaming** response. The caller is responsible for
2611        consuming and closing the response::
2612
2613            response = client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
2614            with response:
2615                for chunk in response.iter_bytes():
2616                    f.write(chunk)
2617
2618        Args:
2619            source_id: Source identifier.
2620            export_id: Export identifier.
2621
2622        Returns:
2623            A streaming ``httpx.Response``. Must be closed by the caller.
2624        """
2625        request = self._client.build_request(
2626            "GET",
2627            f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/download",
2628            headers=_merge_request_headers(options=self._options, request_headers=None),
2629        )
2630        response = self._client.send(request, stream=True)
2631        _raise_for_status(response)
2632        return response
2633
2634    def estimate_source_export(
2635        self, source_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2636    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2637        """Estimate the size/cost of a source export.
2638
2639        Args:
2640            source_id: Source identifier.
2641            body: Export estimation parameters.
2642
2643        Returns:
2644            Export estimate.
2645        """
2646        return cast(
2647            dict[str, Any],
2648            self.request(
2649                "POST",
2650                f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/estimate",
2651                json=body,
2652            ),
2653        )
2654
2655    # ── Source Embedding Migrations ───────────────────────────────────────────
2656
2657    def get_source_embedding_migration(self, source_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
2658        """Get the embedding migration status for a source.

2660        Args:
2661            source_id: Source identifier.
2662
2663        Returns:
2664            Migration status.

2666        return cast(

2671            ),

2676    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

2678

2680            source_id: Source identifier.
2681            body: Migration configuration.
2682
2683        Returns:
2684            The started migration.

2686        return cast(

2691                json=body,
2692            ),
2693        )
2694
2695    def cancel_source_embedding_migration(self, source_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
2696        """Cancel an in-progress embedding migration for a source.
2697
2698        Args:
2699            source_id: Source identifier.
2700
2701        Returns:
2702            The cancelled migration.
2703        """
2704        return cast(
2705            dict[str, Any],
2706            self.request(
2707                "POST",
2708                f"/sources/{source_id}/embedding-migration/cancel",
2709            ),
2710        )

2712    # ── Content (additional) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

2714    def replace_content_with_inline_text(

2735    # ── Solutions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2736
2737    def list_solutions(
2738        self,
2739        *,
2740        page: int = 1,
2741        limit: int = 50,
2742        sort: str = "created_at",
2743        order: str = "desc",
2744    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2745        """List solutions.
2746
2747        Args:
2748            page: Page number (1-indexed).
2749            limit: Items per page.
2750            sort: Sort field.
2751            order: Sort order (``"asc"`` or ``"desc"``).
2752
2753        Returns:
2754            Paginated list of solutions.
2755        """
2756        return cast(
2757            dict[str, Any],
2758            self.request(
2759                "GET",
2760                "/solutions",
2761                params=_strip_none(
2762                    {"page": page, "limit": limit, "sort": sort, "order": order}
2763                ),
2764            ),
2765        )
2766
2767    def create_solution(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
2768        """Create a solution.
2769
2770        Args:
2771            body: Solution creation payload.
2772
2773        Returns:
2774            The created solution.
2775        """
2776        return cast(dict[str, Any], self.request("POST", "/solutions", json=body))
2777
2778    def get_solution(self, solution_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
2779        """Get a solution by ID.
2780
2781        Args:
2782            solution_id: Solution identifier.
2783
2784        Returns:
2785            Solution details.
2786        """
2787        return cast(dict[str, Any], self.request("GET", f"/solutions/{solution_id}"))
2788
2789    def update_solution(self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
2790        """Update a solution.

2792        Args:
2793            solution_id: Solution identifier.
2794            body: Fields to update.
2795
2796        Returns:
2797            The updated solution.
2798        """
2799        return cast(
2800            dict[str, Any],
2801            self.request(
2802                "PATCH",

2804                json=body,
2805            ),
2806        )
2807
2808    def delete_solution(self, solution_id: str) -> None:
2809        """Delete a solution.

2811        Args:

2813        """
2814        self.request("DELETE", f"/solutions/{solution_id}")

2942    def list_solution_conversations(self, solution_id: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:

2958
2959    def add_solution_conversation_turn(
2960        self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2961    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2962        """Add a turn to a solution AI conversation.
2963

2965            solution_id: Solution identifier.

2967

2971        return cast(

2976                json=body,
2977            ),
2978        )
2979
2980    def mark_solution_conversation_turn(
2981        self, solution_id: str, conversation_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2982    ) -> None:
2983        """Mark a solution conversation turn as accepted or rejected.
2984
2985        Args:
2986            solution_id: Solution identifier.
2987            conversation_id: Conversation identifier.
2988            body: Acceptance/rejection payload.
2989        """
2990        self.request(
2991            "PATCH",
2992            f"/solutions/{solution_id}/conversations/{conversation_id}",
2993            json=body,
2994        )
2995
2996    def generate_solution_ai_plan(
2997        self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
2998    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
2999        """Use the AI assistant to generate a solution plan.
3000
3001        Args:
3002            solution_id: Solution identifier.
3003            body: Prompt and context.
3004
3005        Returns:
3006            Generated plan with proposed actions.
3007        """
3008        return cast(
3009            dict[str, Any],
3010            self.request(
3011                "POST",
3012                f"/solutions/{solution_id}/ai-assistant/generate",
3013                json=body,
3014            ),
3015        )
3016
3017    def generate_solution_ai_knowledge_base(
3018        self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
3019    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
3020        """Use the AI assistant to generate a knowledge base plan for a solution.
3021
3022        Args:
3023            solution_id: Solution identifier.
3024            body: Prompt and context.
3025
3026        Returns:
3027            Generated knowledge base plan.
3028        """
3029        return cast(
3030            dict[str, Any],
3031            self.request(
3032                "POST",
3033                f"/solutions/{solution_id}/ai-assistant/knowledge-base",
3034                json=body,
3035            ),
3036        )
3037
3038    def generate_solution_ai_source(
3039        self, solution_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
3040    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
3041        """Use the AI assistant to generate a source plan for a solution.
3042
3043        Args:
3044            solution_id: Solution identifier.
3045            body: Prompt and context.
3046
3047        Returns:
3048            Generated source plan.
3049        """
3050        return cast(
3051            dict[str, Any],

3055                json=body,
3056            ),
3057        )
3058
3059    def accept_solution_ai_plan(
3060        self, solution_id: str, conversation_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]
3061    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
3062        """Accept an AI-generated solution plan.
3063
3064        Args:
3065            solution_id: Solution identifier.
3066            conversation_id: Conversation identifier.
3067            body: Acceptance payload.
3068
3069        Returns:
3070            Result of applying the plan.
3071        """
3072        return cast(
3073            dict[str, Any],
3074            self.request(
3075                "POST",
3076                f"/solutions/{solution_id}/ai-assistant/{conversation_id}/accept",
3077                json=body,
3078            ),
3079        )
3080
3081    def decline_solution_ai_plan(self, solution_id: str, conversation_id: str) -> None:
3082        """Decline an AI-generated solution plan.
3083
3084        Args:
3085            solution_id: Solution identifier.
3086            conversation_id: Conversation identifier.
3087        """
3088        self.request(
3089            "POST",
3090            f"/solutions/{solution_id}/ai-assistant/{conversation_id}/decline",
3091        )
3092

3155    # ── Alerts ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3156
3157    def list_alerts(
3158        self,
3159        *,
3160        page: int = 1,
3161        limit: int = 50,
3162        status: str | None = None,
3163        severity: str | None = None,
3164    ) -> JSONValue:
3165        """List alerts.
3166
3167        Args:
3168            page: Page number (1-indexed).
3169            limit: Items per page.
3170            status: Filter by alert status.
3171            severity: Filter by severity.
3172
3173        Returns:
3174            Paginated list of alerts.
3175        """
3176        return self.request(
3177            "GET",
3178            "/alerts",
3179            params=_strip_none(
3180                {"page": page, "limit": limit, "status": status, "severity": severity}
3181            ),
3182        )
3183
3184    def get_alert(self, alert_id: str) -> JSONValue:
3185        """Get an alert by ID.
3186
3187        Args:
3188            alert_id: Alert identifier.
3189
3190        Returns:
3191            Alert details.
3192        """
3193        return self.request("GET", f"/alerts/{alert_id}")
3194
3195    def change_alert_status(self, alert_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> JSONValue:
3196        """Change the status of an alert.
3197
3198        Args:
3199            alert_id: Alert identifier.
3200            body: New status payload.
3201
3202        Returns:
3203            Updated alert.
3204        """
3205        return self.request("POST", f"/alerts/{alert_id}/status", json=body)
3206
3207    def add_alert_comment(self, alert_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> JSONValue:
3208        """Add a comment to an alert.
3209
3210        Args:
3211            alert_id: Alert identifier.
3212            body: Comment payload.
3213
3214        Returns:
3215            Comment result.
3216        """
3217        return self.request("POST", f"/alerts/{alert_id}/comments", json=body)
3218
3219    def subscribe_to_alert(self, alert_id: str) -> JSONValue:
3220        """Subscribe to an alert.
3221
3222        Args:
3223            alert_id: Alert identifier.
3224
3225        Returns:
3226            Subscription result.
3227        """
3228        return self.request("POST", f"/alerts/{alert_id}/subscribe")
3229
3230    def unsubscribe_from_alert(self, alert_id: str) -> JSONValue:
3231        """Unsubscribe from an alert.
3232
3233        Args:
3234            alert_id: Alert identifier.
3235
3236        Returns:
3237            Unsubscription result.
3238        """
3239        return self.request("POST", f"/alerts/{alert_id}/unsubscribe")
3240
3241    # ── Alert Configs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3242
3243    def list_alert_configs(self, *, page: int = 1, limit: int = 50) -> JSONValue:
3244        """List alert configurations.
3245
3246        Args:
3247            page: Page number (1-indexed).
3248            limit: Items per page.
3249
3250        Returns:
3251            Paginated list of alert configurations.
3252        """
3253        return self.request(
3254            "GET",
3255            "/alerts/configs",
3256            params=_strip_none({"page": page, "limit": limit}),
3257        )
3258
3259    def create_alert_config(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> JSONValue:
3260        """Create an alert configuration.
3261
3262        Args:
3263            body: Alert config creation payload.
3264
3265        Returns:
3266            The created alert configuration.
3267        """
3268        return self.request("POST", "/alerts/configs", json=body)
3269
3270    def get_alert_config(self, config_id: str) -> JSONValue:
3271        """Get an alert configuration by ID.
3272
3273        Args:
3274            config_id: Alert config identifier.
3275
3276        Returns:
3277            Alert configuration details.
3278        """
3279        return self.request("GET", f"/alerts/configs/{config_id}")
3280
3281    def update_alert_config(self, config_id: str, body: dict[str, Any]) -> JSONValue:
3282        """Update an alert configuration.
3283
3284        Args:
3285            config_id: Alert config identifier.
3286            body: Fields to update.
3287
3288        Returns:
3289            The updated alert configuration.
3290        """
3291        return self.request("PATCH", f"/alerts/configs/{config_id}", json=body)
3292
3293    def delete_alert_config(self, config_id: str) -> None:
3294        """Delete an alert configuration.
3295
3296        Args:
3297            config_id: Alert config identifier.
3298        """
3299        self.request("DELETE", f"/alerts/configs/{config_id}")

3427    # ── Model Playground Experiments ──────────────────────────────────────────
3428
3429    def list_experiments(
3430        self,
3431        *,
3432        days: int | None = None,
3433        start_date: str | None = None,
3434        end_date: str | None = None,
3435        limit: int | None = None,
3436        offset: int | None = None,
3437    ) -> JSONValue:
3438        """List model playground experiments.
3439
3440        Args:
3441            days: Look-back window in days (1-730, default 30).
3442            start_date: Start date filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
3443            end_date: End date filter (YYYY-MM-DD).
3444            limit: Maximum number of results.
3445            offset: Pagination offset.
3446
3447        Returns:
3448            Paginated list of experiments.
3449        """
3450        return self.request(
3451            "GET",
3452            "/models/playground/experiments",
3453            params=_strip_none(
3454                {
3455                    "days": days,
3456                    "start_date": start_date,
3457                    "end_date": end_date,
3458                    "limit": limit,
3459                    "offset": offset,
3460                }
3461            ),
3462        )

3497        )

3499    # ── Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

3501    def search(
