3674    # ── Pagination helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3675
3676    def paginate(
3677        self,
3678        method: str,
3679        path: str,
3680        *,
3681        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
3682        limit: int = 50,
3683        items_key: str = "data",
3684    ) -> Generator[dict[str, Any]]:
3685        """Auto-paginate a list endpoint.
3686
3687        Yields individual items from each page.
3688        Stops when a page returns fewer items than ``limit`` or the items list is empty.
3689
3690        Args:
3691            method: HTTP method (typically ``"GET"``).
3692            path: API path.
3693            params: Extra query parameters (``page`` and ``limit`` are managed automatically).
3694            limit: Items per page.
3695            items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default ``"data"``).
3696
3697        Yields:
3698            Individual item dicts from each page.
3699        """
3700        page = 1
3701        base_params = dict(params) if params else {}
3702        while True:
3703            base_params["page"] = page
3704            base_params["limit"] = limit
3705            result = self.request(method, path, params=base_params)
3706            if not isinstance(result, dict):
3707                return
3708            items = result.get(items_key, [])
3709            if not isinstance(items, list):
3710                return
3711            for item in items:
3712                yield cast(dict[str, Any], item)
3713            if len(items) < limit:
3714                return
3715            page += 1
3716
3717    # ── High-level Abstractions ───────────────────────────────────────────────
3718
3719    def run_agent_and_poll(
3720        self,
3721        agent_id: str,
3722        body: AgentRunRequest,
3723        *,
3724        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
3725        include_step_outputs: bool = False,
3726    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
3727        """Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.
3728
3729        Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls ``get_agent_run`` until
3730        the status is ``"completed"`` or ``"failed"``.
3731
3732        Args:
3733            agent_id: Agent identifier.
3734            body: Agent run request payload.
3735            poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0).
3736            include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.
3737
3738        Returns:
3739            The final agent run (completed or failed).
3740
3741        Raises:
3742            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
3743            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
3744        """
3745        run = self.run_agent(agent_id, body)
3746        while run.status not in ("completed", "failed"):
3747            time.sleep(poll_interval)
3748            run = self.get_agent_run(
3749                run.run_id, include_step_outputs=include_step_outputs
3750            )
3751        return run
3752
3753    def run_streaming_agent(
3754        self,
3755        agent_id: str,
3756        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
3757        *,
3758        timeout: float | None = None,
3759        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3760    ) -> Generator[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]:
3761        """Run an agent via SSE streaming, yielding events as they arrive.
3762
3763        This is a generator that yields ``(event_type, data)`` tuples for each
3764        SSE event received. Event types include ``"init"`` and ``"done"``.
3765
3766        Args:
3767            agent_id: Agent identifier.
3768            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
3769            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout.
3770            headers: Optional extra headers.
3771
3772        Yields:
3773            Tuples of ``(event_type, parsed_data)`` for each SSE event.
3774
3775        Raises:
3776            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
3777            SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received.
3778            SeclaiError: If the stream times out.
3779        """
3780        import json as _json
3781
3782        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
3783        merged_headers = _merge_request_headers(
3784            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
3785        )
3786        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
3787        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
3788        start = time.monotonic()
3789
3790        try:
3791            with self._client.stream(
3792                "POST",
3793                path,
3794                json=body,
3795                headers=merged_headers,
3796                timeout=timeout_seconds,
3797            ) as response:
3798                _raise_for_status(response)
3799                current_event: str | None = None
3800                data_lines: list[str] = []
3801
3802                for line in response.iter_lines():
3803                    if time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
3804                        raise SeclaiError(
3805                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
3806                        )
3807                    if line == "":
3808                        if current_event and data_lines:
3809                            data = "\n".join(data_lines)
3810                            try:
3811                                parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
3812                            except Exception:
3813                                parsed = {"raw": data}
3814                            if current_event == "error":
3815                                raise SeclaiStreamingError(
3816                                    parsed.get("message", data),
3817                                    run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
3818                                )
3819                            yield (current_event, parsed)
3820                        current_event = None
3821                        data_lines = []
3822                        continue
3823                    if line.startswith(":"):
3824                        continue
3825                    if line.startswith("event:"):
3826                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
3827                        continue
3828                    if line.startswith("data:"):
3829                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
3830                        continue
3831
3832                # Flush any final event
3833                if current_event and data_lines:
3834                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
3835                    try:
3836                        parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
3837                    except Exception:
3838                        parsed = {"raw": data}
3839                    if current_event == "error":
3840                        raise SeclaiStreamingError(
3841                            parsed.get("message", data),
3842                            run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
3843                        )
3844                    yield (current_event, parsed)
3845        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
3846            raise SeclaiError(
3847                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
3848            ) from e
3849
3850    # ── Upload Helper ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3851
3852    def _prepare_upload_payload(
3853        self,
3854        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
3855        file_name: str | None,
3856        mime_type: str | None,
3857    ) -> tuple[tuple[str | None, BinaryIO, str | None], BinaryIO | None]:
3858        """Prepare a file for multipart upload.
3859
3860        Returns:
3861            A ``(file_tuple, created_handle)`` pair. ``created_handle`` must be
3862            closed by the caller when non-``None``.
3863        """
3864        created: BinaryIO | None = None
3865        if isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
3866            file_path = Path(file)
3867            resolved_name = file_name or file_path.name
3868            resolved_mime = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_name)
3869            created = file_path.open("rb")
3870            return (resolved_name, created, resolved_mime), created
3871        if isinstance(file, bytes):
3872            created = BytesIO(file)
3873            resolved_mime = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
3874            return (file_name, created, resolved_mime), created
3875        resolved_mime = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
3876        return (file_name, file, resolved_mime), None

Seclai synchronous client.

Most users should prefer the typed convenience methods (e.g. run_agent, list_sources) which raise SDK exceptions on failures.

This client can be used as a context manager:

with Seclai(api_key="...") as client:
    sources = client.list_sources()
Seclai( *, api_key: str | None = None, access_token: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None, timeout: float = 30.0, api_key_header: str = 'x-api-key', default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None, http_client: httpx.Client | None = None, profile: str | None = None, config_dir: str | None = None, auto_refresh: bool = True, account_id: str | None = None) 
546    def __init__(
547        self,
548        *,
549        api_key: str | None = None,
550        access_token: str | Callable[[], str] | None = None,
551        timeout: float = 30.0,
552        api_key_header: str = "x-api-key",
553        default_headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
554        http_client: httpx.Client | None = None,
555        profile: str | None = None,
556        config_dir: str | None = None,
557        auto_refresh: bool = True,
558        account_id: str | None = None,
559    ) -> None:
560        """Create a synchronous Seclai client.
561
562        Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):
563
564        1. Explicit ``api_key``
565        2. Explicit ``access_token`` (static string or provider callable)
566        3. ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` environment variable
567        4. SSO profile from ``~/.seclai/config`` + cached tokens
568
569        Args:
570            api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, ``SECLAI_API_KEY`` is used.
571            access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one.
572                Mutually exclusive with ``api_key``.
573            timeout: Request timeout (seconds).
574            api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key.
575            default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request.
576            http_client: Optional pre-configured ``httpx.Client`` to use.
577            profile: SSO profile name from ``~/.seclai/config``.
578            config_dir: Override the config directory path.
579            auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to ``True``.
580            account_id: Target organization account ID (``X-Account-Id`` header).
581
582        Raises:
583            SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.
584        """
585        super().__init__(
586            api_key=api_key,
587            access_token=access_token,
588            timeout=timeout,
589            api_key_header=api_key_header,
590            default_headers=default_headers,
591            profile=profile,
592            config_dir=config_dir,
593            auto_refresh=auto_refresh,
594            account_id=account_id,
595        )
596        self._client = http_client or httpx.Client(
597            base_url=SECLAI_API_URL,
598            timeout=self._options.timeout,
599            headers=self._default_headers(),
600        )
601        self._owns_client = http_client is None

Create a synchronous Seclai client.

Credentials are resolved via a chain (first match wins):

  1. Explicit api_key
  2. Explicit access_token (static string or provider callable)
  3. SECLAI_API_KEY environment variable
  4. SSO profile from ~/.seclai/config + cached tokens

Args: api_key: API key used for authentication. If omitted, SECLAI_API_KEY is used. access_token: Static bearer token string or a callable returning one. Mutually exclusive with api_key. timeout: Request timeout (seconds). api_key_header: Header name to use for the API key. default_headers: Extra headers to include on every request. http_client: Optional pre-configured httpx.Client to use. profile: SSO profile name from ~/.seclai/config. config_dir: Override the config directory path. auto_refresh: Auto-refresh expired SSO tokens. Defaults to True. account_id: Target organization account ID (X-Account-Id header).

Raises: SeclaiConfigurationError: If no credentials are found.

def close(self) -> None: 
603    def close(self) -> None:
604        """Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.
605
606        If you created the client with a custom ``http_client``, this is a no-op
607        for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).
608        """
609        if self._owns_client:
610            self._client.close()
611        if self._owns_generated_client and self._generated_client_instance is not None:
612            self._generated_client_instance.get_httpx_client().close()

Close underlying HTTP resources owned by this client.

If you created the client with a custom http_client, this is a no-op for that client (you remain responsible for closing it).

def request( self, method: str, path: str, *, params: Mapping[str, typing.Any] | None = None, json: dict[str, dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | list[dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | str | int | float | bool | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> dict[str, dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | list[dict[str, ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | list[ForwardRef('JSONValue')] | str | int | float | bool | None] | str | int | float | bool | None: 
628    def request(
629        self,
630        method: str,
631        path: str,
632        *,
633        params: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
634        json: JSONValue | None = None,
635        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
636    ) -> JSONValue | str | None:
637        """Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.
638
639        This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed
640        convenience methods on this client.
641
642        Args:
643            method: HTTP method (e.g. `"GET"`, `"POST"`).
644            path: Request path relative to `SECLAI_API_URL` (e.g. `"/sources/"`).
645            params: Query parameters.
646            json: JSON body to send.
647            headers: Per-request header overrides.
648
649        Returns:
650            Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or `None` for empty bodies.
651
652        Raises:
653            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.
654        """
655        response = self._client.request(
656            method,
657            path,
658            params=params,
659            json=json,
660            headers=_merge_request_headers(
661                options=self._options, request_headers=headers
662            ),
663        )
664        _raise_for_status(response)
665        if not response.content:
666            return None
667        content_type = response.headers.get("content-type", "")
668        if "application/json" in content_type:
669            return cast(JSONValue, response.json())
670        return response.text

Make a raw HTTP request to the Seclai API.

This is a low-level escape hatch. For most operations, prefer the typed convenience methods on this client.

Args: method: HTTP method (e.g. "GET", "POST"). path: Request path relative to SECLAI_API_URL (e.g. "/sources/"). params: Query parameters. json: JSON body to send. headers: Per-request header overrides.

Returns: Parsed JSON for JSON responses, raw text for non-JSON responses, or None for empty bodies.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the response status code is not successful.

673        """Run an agent.

689            sync_detailed,

694            agent_id=agent_id, client=self._sync_generated_client(), body=body
695        )

697            method="POST",

699            response=response,
700            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
701        )
702        if response.parsed is None:

704                message="Empty response",
705                status_code=int(response.status_code),
706                method="POST",
707                url=self._build_url(path),

714                status_code=int(response.status_code),
715                method="POST",
716                url=self._build_url(path),
717                response_text=None,
718                validation_error=parsed,
719            )
720        return parsed

Run an agent.

Starts a new agent run for the given agent and returns the run record. You'll need the agent run ID to track the run's progress and fetch the result of completed runs.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Agent run request payload.

Returns: The created agent run.

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

def run_streaming_agent_and_wait( self, agent_id: str, body: AgentRunStreamRequest, *, timeout: float | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> seclai._generated.models.agent_run_response.AgentRunResponse: 
722    def run_streaming_agent_and_wait(
723        self,
724        agent_id: str,
725        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
726        *,
727        timeout: float | None = None,
728        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
729    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
730        """Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion.
731
732        This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE).
733        The method returns when an `event: done` message is received.
734
735        Args:
736            agent_id: Agent identifier.
737            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
738            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait for the final result (client-side). Defaults to this
739                client's configured timeout.
740            headers: Optional extra headers for this request.
741
742        Returns:
743            The final agent run payload from the `done` event.
744
745        Raises:
746            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
747            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
748            SeclaiError: If the stream ends early or times out.
749        """
750        import json as _json
751        import time as _time
752
753        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
754
755        merged_headers = _merge_request_headers(
756            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
757        )
758        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
759
760        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
761        start = _time.monotonic()
762
763        try:
764            with self._client.stream(
765                "POST",
766                path,
767                json=body,
768                headers=merged_headers,
769                timeout=timeout_seconds,
770            ) as response:
771                if response.status_code == HTTPStatus.UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY:
772                    payload = response.json()
773                    raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
774                        message=f"Request failed with status {response.status_code}",
775                        status_code=int(response.status_code),
776                        method="POST",
777                        url=self._build_url(path),
778                        response_text=response.text,
779                        validation_error=HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload),
780                    )
781                _raise_for_status(response)
782
783                current_event: str | None = None
784                data_lines: list[str] = []
785                last_seen: AgentRunResponse | None = None
786
787                def dispatch() -> AgentRunResponse | None:
788                    nonlocal current_event, data_lines, last_seen
789                    if current_event is None and not data_lines:
790                        return None
791                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
792                    evt = current_event
793                    current_event = None
794                    data_lines = []
795                    if not data:
796                        return None
797                    if evt not in {"init", "done"}:
798                        return None
799                    try:
800                        parsed_dict = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
801                        parsed = AgentRunResponse.from_dict(parsed_dict)
802                        last_seen = parsed
803                        if evt == "done":
804                            return parsed
805                    except Exception:
806                        return None
807                    return None
808
809                for line in response.iter_lines():
810                    if _time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
811                        raise SeclaiError(
812                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
813                        )
814
815                    if line == "":
816                        done = dispatch()
817                        if done is not None:
818                            return done
819                        continue
820                    if line.startswith(":"):
821                        continue
822                    if line.startswith("event:"):
823                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
824                        continue
825                    if line.startswith("data:"):
826                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
827                        continue
828
829                done = dispatch()
830                if done is not None:
831                    return done
832                if last_seen is not None:
833                    return last_seen
834                raise SeclaiError("Stream ended before receiving a 'done' event.")
835        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
836            raise SeclaiError(
837                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run to complete."
838            ) from e

Run an agent via SSE streaming and block until completion.

This uses POST /agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream and consumes Server-Sent Events (SSE). The method returns when an event: done message is received.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Streaming agent run request payload. timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait for the final result (client-side). Defaults to this client's configured timeout. headers: Optional extra headers for this request.

Returns: The final agent run payload from the done event.

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code. SeclaiError: If the stream ends early or times out.

909        """Get details of a specific agent run.

914            run_id: Run identifier.

917                durations, and credits. Defaults to False.

925        """

927            run_id = args[0]

930            run_id = args[1]

932            raise TypeError("get_agent_run expects (run_id) or (agent_id, run_id)")

941            client=self._sync_generated_client(),
942            include_step_outputs=include_step_outputs,
943        )

945            method="GET",
946            path=path,
947            response=response,
948            ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},
949        )
950        if response.parsed is None:
951            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
952                message="Empty response",
953                status_code=int(response.status_code),
954                method="GET",
955                url=self._build_url(path),
956                response_text=None,
957            )

968        return parsed

1205            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
1206            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

1214            client=self._sync_generated_client(),
1215            page=page,
1216            limit=limit,
1217            sort=sort,
1218            order=order,
1219            account_id=account_id,
1220        )
1221        self._raise_for_openapi_response(
1222            method="GET",
1223            path=path,
1224            response=response,

1227        if response.parsed is None:
1228            raise SeclaiAPIStatusError(
1229                message="Empty response",

1231                method="GET",
1232                url=self._build_url(path),

1234            )
1235        parsed = response.parsed
1236        if isinstance(parsed, HTTPValidationError):
1237            raise SeclaiAPIValidationError(
1238                message="Validation error",
1239                status_code=int(response.status_code),
1240                method="GET",
1241                url=self._build_url(path),
1242                response_text=None,
1243                validation_error=parsed,
1244            )
1245        return parsed

List sources.

Returns the sources visible to your account (optionally filtered by account_id).

Args: page: Page number (1-indexed). limit: Items per page. sort: Sort field. order: Sort order (e.g. "asc" or "desc"). account_id: Optional account id; defaults to the API key's account.

Returns: A paginated list of sources.

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

1258
1259        Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.
1260
1261        Maximum file size: 200 MiB.
1262
1263        Supported MIME types:
1264            - `application/epub+zip`
1265            - `application/json`
1266            - `application/msword`
1267            - `application/pdf`
1268            - `application/vnd.ms-excel`
1269            - `application/vnd.ms-outlook`
1270            - `application/vnd.ms-powerpoint`
1271            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation`
1272            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet`
1273            - `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`
1274            - `application/xml`
1275            - `application/zip`
1276            - `audio/flac`
1277            - `audio/mp4`
1278            - `audio/mpeg`
1279            - `audio/ogg`
1280            - `audio/wav`
1281            - `image/bmp`
1282            - `image/gif`
1283            - `image/jpeg`
1284            - `image/png`
1285            - `image/tiff`
1286            - `image/webp`
1287            - `text/csv`
1288            - `text/html`
1289            - `text/markdown` / `text/x-markdown`
1290            - `text/plain`
1291            - `text/xml`
1292            - `video/mp4`
1293            - `video/quicktime`
1294            - `video/x-msvideo`
1295
1296        If `mime_type` is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it.
1297        If the upload is sent as `application/octet-stream`, the server attempts to infer
1298        the type from the file extension.
1299
1300        Notes:
1301            - If `file` is a path or `bytes`, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and
1302              closes it before returning.
1303            - If `file` is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.
1304
1305        Args:
1306            source_connection_id: Source connection identifier.
1307            file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like.
1308            title: Optional title for the uploaded file.
1309            metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content.
1310            file_name: Optional filename to send when `file` is bytes or a file-like.

1314            Upload response details.

1318            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

1329            upload_file: File

1332            elif isinstance(file, (str, os.PathLike)):
1333                file_path = Path(file)
1334                resolved_file_name = file_name or file_path.name
1335                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(resolved_file_name)
1336                created_payload = file_path.open("rb")
1337                upload_file = File(
1338                    payload=created_payload,
1339                    file_name=resolved_file_name,
1340                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1341                )
1342            elif isinstance(file, bytes):
1343                created_payload = BytesIO(file)
1344                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1345                upload_file = File(
1346                    payload=created_payload,
1347                    file_name=file_name,
1348                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1349                )
1350            else:
1351                resolved_mime_type = mime_type or _guess_mime_type(file_name)
1352                upload_file = File(
1353                    payload=file,
1354                    file_name=file_name,
1355                    mime_type=resolved_mime_type,
1356                )
1357

1359                file=upload_file,
1360                title=UNSET if title is None else title,
1361            )
1362
1363            if metadata is not None:
1364                body["metadata"] = _json.dumps(metadata)
1365
1366            endpoint_path = f"/sources/{source_connection_id}/upload"
1367
1368            # Note: openapi-python-client currently struggles with Seclai's spec for this endpoint
1369            # due to duplicate schema names, so we send the multipart request directly and parse
1370            # into our SDK model types.
1371            http = self._sync_generated_client().get_httpx_client()

1375                files=body.to_multipart(),
1376            )
1377

1379            if raw.status_code == 200:
1380                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
1381                parsed = FileUploadResponse.from_dict(payload)
1382            elif raw.status_code == 422:
1383                payload = cast(dict[str, Any], raw.json())
1384                parsed = HTTPValidationError.from_dict(payload)
1385
1386            response = OpenAPIResponse(
1387                status_code=HTTPStatus(raw.status_code),
1388                content=raw.content,
1389                headers=raw.headers,
1390                parsed=parsed,
1391            )
1392            self._raise_for_openapi_response(
1393                method="POST",
1394                path=endpoint_path,
1395                response=response,
1396                ok_statuses={HTTPStatus.OK},

1400                    message="Empty response",

1416                    validation_error=parsed_response,

Upload a file to a specific source connection.

Sends a file to be ingested under the given source connection.

Maximum file size: 200 MiB.

Supported MIME types: - application/epub+zip - application/json - application/msword - application/pdf - application/vnd.ms-excel - application/vnd.ms-outlook - application/vnd.ms-powerpoint - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.presentationml.presentation - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet - application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document - application/xml - application/zip - audio/flac - audio/mp4 - audio/mpeg - audio/ogg - audio/wav - image/bmp - image/gif - image/jpeg - image/png - image/tiff - image/webp - text/csv - text/html - text/markdown / text/x-markdown - text/plain - text/xml - video/mp4 - video/quicktime - video/x-msvideo

If mime_type is omitted and a filename is available, the SDK tries to infer it. If the upload is sent as application/octet-stream, the server attempts to infer the type from the file extension.

Notes: - If file is a path or bytes, this method creates an in-memory/file handle and closes it before returning. - If file is a file-like object, the caller owns its lifecycle.

Args: source_connection_id: Source connection identifier. file: File payload. Accepts raw bytes, a filesystem path, or a binary file-like. title: Optional title for the uploaded file. metadata: Optional metadata dictionary to associate with the uploaded content. file_name: Optional filename to send when file is bytes or a file-like. mime_type: Optional MIME type to send.

Returns: Upload response details.

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

Replace an existing content version with a new file upload.

This uploads a new file to /contents/{source_connection_content_version}/upload and replaces the content backing that existing content version.

Export an agent definition as a portable JSON snapshot.

def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
1625    def preview_import_agent(self, body: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1626        """Validate an ``agent_definition`` payload without creating an agent.
1627
1628        Use before :meth:`create_agent` or :meth:`update_agent` with an
1629        ``agent_definition`` to surface ``unresolved_refs`` — workflow
1630        references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or
1631        sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned
1632        ids back in ``entity_remap`` on the commit call to substitute them.
1633
1634        Args:
1635            body: ``{"agent_definition": ...}`` — same shape produced by
1636                :meth:`export_agent`.
1637
1638        Returns:
1639            Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any
1640            ``unresolved_refs``.
1641
1642        Raises:
1643            SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including
1644                HTTP 422 when the ``agent_definition`` fails validation.
1645                On 422 the response body (available as
1646                ``SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text``) is an
1647                ``AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse`` listing each field
1648                error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical
1649                ``source`` echo.
1650        """
1651        return cast(
1652            dict[str, Any],
1653            self.request("POST", "/agents/preview-import", json=body),
1654        )

Validate an agent_definition payload without creating an agent.

Use before create_agent() or update_agent() with an agent_definition to surface unresolved_refs — workflow references to knowledge bases, memory banks, source connections, or sub-agents that don't exist in the target account. Pass the returned ids back in entity_remap on the commit call to substitute them.

Args: body: {"agent_definition": ...} — same shape produced by export_agent().

Returns: Summary with step/schedule/alert/criteria/policy counts and any unresolved_refs.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: For any non-success response, including HTTP 422 when the agent_definition fails validation. On 422 the response body (available as SeclaiAPIStatusError.response_text) is an AgentDefinitionImportErrorResponse listing each field error with a 1-indexed line/column anchored to the canonical source echo.

1668            dict[str, Any], self.request("GET", f"/agents/{agent_id}/definition")
1669        )

Get the step definition for an agent.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier.

Returns: The agent's step definition.

1690        )

1726        file: bytes | str | os.PathLike[str] | BinaryIO,
1727        file_name: str | None = None,
1728        mime_type: str | None = None,
1729    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1730        """Upload a file as input for an agent run.
1731
1732        Args:
1733            agent_id: Agent identifier.
1734            file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like).
1735            file_name: Filename to send.
1736            mime_type: MIME type of the file.
1737
1738        Returns:
1739            Upload response with the input upload ID.
1740        """
1741        payload, created = self._prepare_upload_payload(file, file_name, mime_type)
1742        try:
1743            response = self._client.post(
1744                f"/agents/{agent_id}/upload-input",
1745                files={"file": payload},
1746                headers=_merge_request_headers(
1747                    options=self._options, request_headers=None
1748                ),
1749            )
1750            _raise_for_status(response)
1751            return cast(dict[str, Any], response.json())
1752        finally:
1753            if created is not None:
1754                created.close()

Upload a file as input for an agent run.

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. file: File payload (bytes, path, or file-like). file_name: Filename to send. mime_type: MIME type of the file.

Returns: Upload response with the input upload ID.

2317            List of agents using this memory bank.
2318        """
2319        return self.request("GET", f"/memory_banks/{memory_bank_id}/agents")

def download_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> httpx.Response: 
2607    def download_source_export(self, source_id: str, export_id: str) -> httpx.Response:
2608        """Download a completed source export.
2609
2610        Returns a **streaming** response. The caller is responsible for
2611        consuming and closing the response::
2612
2613            response = client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
2614            with response:
2615                for chunk in response.iter_bytes():
2616                    f.write(chunk)
2617
2618        Args:
2619            source_id: Source identifier.
2620            export_id: Export identifier.
2621
2622        Returns:
2623            A streaming ``httpx.Response``. Must be closed by the caller.
2624        """
2625        request = self._client.build_request(
2626            "GET",
2627            f"/sources/{source_id}/exports/{export_id}/download",
2628            headers=_merge_request_headers(options=self._options, request_headers=None),
2629        )
2630        response = self._client.send(request, stream=True)
2631        _raise_for_status(response)
2632        return response

Download a completed source export.

Returns a streaming response. The caller is responsible for consuming and closing the response::

response = client.download_source_export("src", "exp")
with response:
    for chunk in response.iter_bytes():
        f.write(chunk)

Args: source_id: Source identifier. export_id: Export identifier.

Returns: A streaming httpx.Response. Must be closed by the caller.

3157    def list_alerts(
3158        self,
3159        *,
3160        page: int = 1,
3161        limit: int = 50,

3164    ) -> JSONValue:
3165        """List alerts.
3166

3174            Paginated list of alerts.
3175        """
3176        return self.request(
3177            "GET",
3178            "/alerts",
3179            params=_strip_none(
3180                {"page": page, "limit": limit, "status": status, "severity": severity}
3181            ),
3182        )

3398            supports_tool_use: Filter to models that support tool use.
3399            supports_thinking: Filter to models that support extended thinking.
3400
3401        Returns:
3402            List of provider groups with their models.
3403        """
3404        return self.request(
3405            "GET",
3406            "/models",
3407            params=_strip_none(
3408                {
3409                    "provider": provider,
3410                    "supports_tool_use": supports_tool_use,
3411                    "supports_thinking": supports_thinking,
3412                }
3413            ),
3414        )

List all enabled LLM models grouped by provider.

Args: provider: Filter by provider name. supports_tool_use: Filter to models that support tool use. supports_thinking: Filter to models that support extended thinking.

Returns: List of provider groups with their models.

def paginate( self, method: str, path: str, *, params: dict[str, typing.Any] | None = None, limit: int = 50, items_key: str = 'data') -> Generator[dict[str, typing.Any]]: 
3676    def paginate(
3677        self,
3678        method: str,
3679        path: str,
3680        *,
3681        params: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
3682        limit: int = 50,
3683        items_key: str = "data",
3684    ) -> Generator[dict[str, Any]]:
3685        """Auto-paginate a list endpoint.
3686
3687        Yields individual items from each page.
3688        Stops when a page returns fewer items than ``limit`` or the items list is empty.
3689
3690        Args:
3691            method: HTTP method (typically ``"GET"``).
3692            path: API path.
3693            params: Extra query parameters (``page`` and ``limit`` are managed automatically).
3694            limit: Items per page.
3695            items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default ``"data"``).
3696
3697        Yields:
3698            Individual item dicts from each page.
3699        """
3700        page = 1
3701        base_params = dict(params) if params else {}
3702        while True:
3703            base_params["page"] = page
3704            base_params["limit"] = limit
3705            result = self.request(method, path, params=base_params)
3706            if not isinstance(result, dict):
3707                return
3708            items = result.get(items_key, [])
3709            if not isinstance(items, list):
3710                return
3711            for item in items:
3712                yield cast(dict[str, Any], item)
3713            if len(items) < limit:
3714                return
3715            page += 1

Auto-paginate a list endpoint.

Yields individual items from each page. Stops when a page returns fewer items than limit or the items list is empty.

Args: method: HTTP method (typically "GET"). path: API path. params: Extra query parameters (page and limit are managed automatically). limit: Items per page. items_key: JSON key containing the list of items (default "data").

Yields: Individual item dicts from each page.

def run_agent_and_poll( self, agent_id: str, body: seclai._generated.models.agent_run_request.AgentRunRequest, *, poll_interval: float = 2.0, include_step_outputs: bool = False) -> seclai._generated.models.agent_run_response.AgentRunResponse: 
3719    def run_agent_and_poll(
3720        self,
3721        agent_id: str,
3722        body: AgentRunRequest,
3723        *,
3724        poll_interval: float = 2.0,
3725        include_step_outputs: bool = False,
3726    ) -> AgentRunResponse:
3727        """Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.
3728
3729        Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls ``get_agent_run`` until
3730        the status is ``"completed"`` or ``"failed"``.
3731
3732        Args:
3733            agent_id: Agent identifier.
3734            body: Agent run request payload.
3735            poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0).
3736            include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.
3737
3738        Returns:
3739            The final agent run (completed or failed).
3740
3741        Raises:
3742            SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error.
3743            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
3744        """
3745        run = self.run_agent(agent_id, body)
3746        while run.status not in ("completed", "failed"):
3747            time.sleep(poll_interval)
3748            run = self.get_agent_run(
3749                run.run_id, include_step_outputs=include_step_outputs
3750            )
3751        return run

Run an agent and poll until it reaches a terminal status.

Starts the agent run and then repeatedly polls get_agent_run until the status is "completed" or "failed".

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Agent run request payload. poll_interval: Seconds between poll attempts (default 2.0). include_step_outputs: Include per-step outputs when polling.

Returns: The final agent run (completed or failed).

Raises: SeclaiAPIValidationError: If the API returns a validation error. SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.

def run_streaming_agent( self, agent_id: str, body: AgentRunStreamRequest, *, timeout: float | None = None, headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None) -> Generator[tuple[str, dict[str, typing.Any]]]: 
3753    def run_streaming_agent(
3754        self,
3755        agent_id: str,
3756        body: AgentRunStreamRequest,
3757        *,
3758        timeout: float | None = None,
3759        headers: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
3760    ) -> Generator[tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]]:
3761        """Run an agent via SSE streaming, yielding events as they arrive.
3762
3763        This is a generator that yields ``(event_type, data)`` tuples for each
3764        SSE event received. Event types include ``"init"`` and ``"done"``.
3765
3766        Args:
3767            agent_id: Agent identifier.
3768            body: Streaming agent run request payload.
3769            timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout.
3770            headers: Optional extra headers.
3771
3772        Yields:
3773            Tuples of ``(event_type, parsed_data)`` for each SSE event.
3774
3775        Raises:
3776            SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code.
3777            SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received.
3778            SeclaiError: If the stream times out.
3779        """
3780        import json as _json
3781
3782        path = f"/agents/{agent_id}/runs/stream"
3783        merged_headers = _merge_request_headers(
3784            options=self._options, request_headers=headers
3785        )
3786        merged_headers.setdefault("accept", "text/event-stream")
3787        timeout_seconds = self._options.timeout if timeout is None else timeout
3788        start = time.monotonic()
3789
3790        try:
3791            with self._client.stream(
3792                "POST",
3793                path,
3794                json=body,
3795                headers=merged_headers,
3796                timeout=timeout_seconds,
3797            ) as response:
3798                _raise_for_status(response)
3799                current_event: str | None = None
3800                data_lines: list[str] = []
3801
3802                for line in response.iter_lines():
3803                    if time.monotonic() - start > timeout_seconds:
3804                        raise SeclaiError(
3805                            f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
3806                        )
3807                    if line == "":
3808                        if current_event and data_lines:
3809                            data = "\n".join(data_lines)
3810                            try:
3811                                parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
3812                            except Exception:
3813                                parsed = {"raw": data}
3814                            if current_event == "error":
3815                                raise SeclaiStreamingError(
3816                                    parsed.get("message", data),
3817                                    run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
3818                                )
3819                            yield (current_event, parsed)
3820                        current_event = None
3821                        data_lines = []
3822                        continue
3823                    if line.startswith(":"):
3824                        continue
3825                    if line.startswith("event:"):
3826                        current_event = line[len("event:") :].strip() or None
3827                        continue
3828                    if line.startswith("data:"):
3829                        data_lines.append(line[len("data:") :].lstrip())
3830                        continue
3831
3832                # Flush any final event
3833                if current_event and data_lines:
3834                    data = "\n".join(data_lines)
3835                    try:
3836                        parsed = cast(dict[str, Any], _json.loads(data))
3837                    except Exception:
3838                        parsed = {"raw": data}
3839                    if current_event == "error":
3840                        raise SeclaiStreamingError(
3841                            parsed.get("message", data),
3842                            run_id=parsed.get("run_id"),
3843                        )
3844                    yield (current_event, parsed)
3845        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:
3846            raise SeclaiError(
3847                f"Timed out after {timeout_seconds}s waiting for streaming agent run."
3848            ) from e

Run an agent via SSE streaming, yielding events as they arrive.

This is a generator that yields (event_type, data) tuples for each SSE event received. Event types include "init" and "done".

Args: agent_id: Agent identifier. body: Streaming agent run request payload. timeout: Max time (seconds) to wait. Defaults to this client's timeout. headers: Optional extra headers.

Yields: Tuples of (event_type, parsed_data) for each SSE event.

Raises: SeclaiAPIStatusError: If the API returns a non-success status code. SeclaiStreamingError: If an error event is received. SeclaiError: If the stream times out.

class SeclaiAPIStatusError(seclai.SeclaiError): 
108class SeclaiAPIStatusError(SeclaiError):
109    """Raised when the Seclai API returns a non-success status code.
110
111    Attributes:
112        status_code: HTTP status code returned by the API.
113        method: HTTP method used for the request.
114        url: Full request URL.
115        response_text: Best-effort response body text, if available.
116    """
117
118    def __init__(
119        self,
120        *,
121        message: str,
122        status_code: int,
123        method: str,
124        url: str,
125        response_text: str | None,
126    ) -> None:
127        """Create a status error for a non-2xx API response."""
128        super().__init__(message)
129        self.message = message
130        self.status_code = status_code
131        self.method = method
132        self.url = url
133        self.response_text = response_text

Raised when the Seclai API returns a non-success status code.

Attributes: status_code: HTTP status code returned by the API. method: HTTP method used for the request. url: Full request URL. response_text: Best-effort response body text, if available.

class SeclaiAPIValidationError(seclai.SeclaiAPIStatusError): 
136class SeclaiAPIValidationError(SeclaiAPIStatusError):
137    """Raised when the API returns a validation error response (typically HTTP 422).
138
139    Attributes:
140        validation_error: Parsed validation details returned by the API.
141    """
142
143    def __init__(
144        self,
145        *,
146        message: str,
147        status_code: int,
148        method: str,
149        url: str,
150        response_text: str | None,
151        validation_error: HTTPValidationError,
152    ) -> None:
153        """Initialize with validation error details."""
154        super().__init__(
155            message=message,
156            status_code=status_code,
157            method=method,
158            url=url,
159            response_text=response_text,
160        )
161        self.validation_error = validation_error

Raised when the API returns a validation error response (typically HTTP 422).

Attributes: validation_error: Parsed validation details returned by the API.

class SeclaiStreamingError(seclai.SeclaiError): 
164class SeclaiStreamingError(SeclaiError):
165    """Raised when an error event is received during a streaming agent run.
166
167    Attributes:
168        message: Human-readable error message from the stream.
169        run_id: Agent run identifier, if available.
170    """
171
172    def __init__(self, message: str, *, run_id: str | None = None) -> None:
173        """Initialize with stream error message and optional run ID."""
174        super().__init__(message)
175        self.message = message
176        self.run_id = run_id

Raised when an error event is received during a streaming agent run.

Attributes: message: Human-readable error message from the stream. run_id: Agent run identifier, if available.

@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class SsoProfile: 
50@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
51class SsoProfile:
52    """Resolved SSO profile settings from the config file.
53
54    Attributes:
55        sso_account_id: Account ID (resolved after login via ``/me``).
56        sso_region: AWS region for the Cognito user pool.
57        sso_client_id: Cognito app client ID.
58        sso_domain: Cognito domain (e.g. ``"auth.example.com"``).
59    """
60
61    sso_region: str
62    sso_client_id: str
63    sso_domain: str
64    sso_account_id: str | None = None

Resolved SSO profile settings from the config file.

Attributes: sso_account_id: Account ID (resolved after login via /me). sso_region: AWS region for the Cognito user pool. sso_client_id: Cognito app client ID. sso_domain: Cognito domain (e.g. "auth.example.com").

SsoProfile( sso_region: str, sso_client_id: str, sso_domain: str, sso_account_id: str | None = None)

@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
class SsoCacheEntry: 
 67@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
 68class SsoCacheEntry:
 69    """Contents of a single SSO cache file.
 70
 71    Attributes:
 72        access_token: JWT access token.
 73        refresh_token: Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens.
 74        id_token: OIDC ID token (optional).
 75        expires_at: ISO-8601 expiry timestamp for the access token.
 76        client_id: Cognito app client ID.
 77        region: AWS region.
 78        cognito_domain: Cognito domain.
 79    """
 80
 81    access_token: str
 82    refresh_token: str | None
 83    id_token: str | None
 84    expires_at: str  # ISO-8601
 85    client_id: str
 86    region: str
 87    cognito_domain: str
 88
 89    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
 90        """Serialize to a JSON-compatible dict with camelCase keys."""
 91        d: dict[str, Any] = {
 92            "accessToken": self.access_token,
 93            "expiresAt": self.expires_at,
 94            "clientId": self.client_id,
 95            "region": self.region,
 96            "cognitoDomain": self.cognito_domain,
 97        }
 98        if self.refresh_token is not None:
 99            d["refreshToken"] = self.refresh_token
100        if self.id_token is not None:
101            d["idToken"] = self.id_token
102        return d
103
104    @classmethod
105    def from_dict(cls, data: dict[str, Any]) -> SsoCacheEntry:
106        """Deserialize from a JSON dict with camelCase keys."""
107        return cls(
108            access_token=data["accessToken"],
109            refresh_token=data.get("refreshToken"),
110            id_token=data.get("idToken"),
111            expires_at=data["expiresAt"],
112            client_id=data["clientId"],
113            region=data["region"],
114            cognito_domain=data["cognitoDomain"],
115        )

Contents of a single SSO cache file.

Attributes: access_token: JWT access token. refresh_token: Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens. id_token: OIDC ID token (optional). expires_at: ISO-8601 expiry timestamp for the access token. client_id: Cognito app client ID. region: AWS region. cognito_domain: Cognito domain.

SsoCacheEntry( access_token: str, refresh_token: str | None, id_token: str | None, expires_at: str, client_id: str, region: str, cognito_domain: str)
